units.text: Enter two Gentlemen

units.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
units.text:
You do not meet a man but frowns: our bloods
No more obey the heavens than our courtiers
Still seem as does the king.

units.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
units.text: But what's the matter?

units.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
units.text:
His daughter, and the heir of's kingdom, whom
He purposed to his wife's sole son—a widow
That late he married—hath referr'd herself
Unto a poor but worthy gentleman: she's wedded;
Her husband banish'd; she imprison'd: all
Is outward sorrow; though I think the king
Be touch'd at very heart.

units.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
units.text: None but the king?

units.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
units.text:
He that hath lost her too; so is the queen,
That most desired the match; but not a courtier,
Although they wear their faces to the bent
Of the king's look's, hath a heart that is not
Glad at the thing they scowl at.

units.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
units.text: And why so?

units.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
units.text:
He that hath miss'd the princess is a thing
Too bad for bad report: and he that hath her—
I mean, that married her, alack, good man!
And therefore banish'd—is a creature such
As, to seek through the regions of the earth
For one his like, there would be something failing
In him that should compare. I do not think
So fair an outward and such stuff within
Endows a man but he.

units.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
units.text: You speak him far.

units.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
units.text:
I do extend him, sir, within himself,
Crush him together rather than unfold
His measure duly.

units.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
units.text: What's his name and birth?

units.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
units.text:
I cannot delve him to the root: his father
Was call'd Sicilius, who did join his honour
Against the Romans with Cassibelan,
But had his titles by Tenantius whom
He served with glory and admired success,
So gain'd the sur-addition Leonatus;
And had, besides this gentleman in question,
Two other sons, who in the wars o' the time
Died with their swords in hand; for which
their father,
Then old and fond of issue, took such sorrow
That he quit being, and his gentle lady,
Big of this gentleman our theme, deceased
As he was born. The king he takes the babe
To his protection, calls him Posthumus Leonatus,
Breeds him and makes him of his bed-chamber,
Puts to him all the learnings that his time
Could make him the receiver of; which he took,
As we do air, fast as 'twas minister'd,
And in's spring became a harvest, lived in court—
Which rare it is to do—most praised, most loved,
A sample to the youngest, to the more mature
A glass that feated them, and to the graver
A child that guided dotards; to his mistress,
For whom he now is banish'd, her own price
Proclaims how she esteem'd him and his virtue;
By her election may be truly read
What kind of man he is.

units.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
units.text:
I honour him
Even out of your report. But, pray you, tell me,
Is she sole child to the king?

units.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
units.text:
His only child.
He had two sons: if this be worth your hearing,
Mark it: the eldest of them at three years old,
I' the swathing-clothes the other, from their nursery
Were stol'n, and to this hour no guess in knowledge
Which way they went.

units.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
units.text: How long is this ago?

units.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
units.text: Some twenty years.

units.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
units.text:
That a king's children should be so convey'd,
So slackly guarded, and the search so slow,
That could not trace them!

units.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
units.text:
Howsoe'er 'tis strange,
Or that the negligence may well be laugh'd at,
Yet is it true, sir.

units.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
units.text: I do well believe you.

units.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
units.text:
We must forbear: here comes the gentleman,
The queen, and princess.
Exeunt
Enter the QUEEN, POSTHUMUS LEONATUS, and IMOGEN

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
No, be assured you shall not find me, daughter,
After the slander of most stepmothers,
Evil-eyed unto you: you're my prisoner, but
Your gaoler shall deliver you the keys
That lock up your restraint. For you, Posthumus,
So soon as I can win the offended king,
I will be known your advocate: marry, yet
The fire of rage is in him, and 'twere good
You lean'd unto his sentence with what patience
Your wisdom may inform you.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Please your highness,
I will from hence to-day.

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
You know the peril.
I'll fetch a turn about the garden, pitying
The pangs of barr'd affections, though the king
Hath charged you should not speak together.
Exit

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
O
Dissembling courtesy! How fine this tyrant
Can tickle where she wounds! My dearest husband,
I something fear my father's wrath; but nothing—
Always reserved my holy duty—what
His rage can do on me: you must be gone;
And I shall here abide the hourly shot
Of angry eyes, not comforted to live,
But that there is this jewel in the world
That I may see again.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
My queen! my mistress!
O lady, weep no more, lest I give cause
To be suspected of more tenderness
Than doth become a man. I will remain
The loyal'st husband that did e'er plight troth:
My residence in Rome at one Philario's,
Who to my father was a friend, to me
Known but by letter: thither write, my queen,
And with mine eyes I'll drink the words you send,
Though ink be made of gall.
Re-enter QUEEN

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
Be brief, I pray you:
If the king come, I shall incur I know not
How much of his displeasure.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Should we be taking leave
As long a term as yet we have to live,
The loathness to depart would grow. Adieu!

units.speaker: IMOGEN
POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
How, how! another?
You gentle gods, give me but this I have,
And sear up my embracements from a next
With bonds of death!
Putting on the ring

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Remain, remain thou here
While sense can keep it on. And, sweetest, fairest,
As I my poor self did exchange for you,
To your so infinite loss, so in our trifles
I still win of you: for my sake wear this;
It is a manacle of love; I'll place it
Upon this fairest prisoner.
Putting a bracelet upon her arm

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
O the gods!
When shall we see again?
Enter CYMBELINE and Lords

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text: Alack, the king!

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text:
Thou basest thing, avoid! hence, from my sight!
If after this command thou fraught the court
With thy unworthiness, thou diest: away!
Thou'rt poison to my blood.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
The gods protect you!
And bless the good remainders of the court! I am gone.
Exit

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
There cannot be a pinch in death
More sharp than this is.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text:
O disloyal thing,
That shouldst repair my youth, thou heap'st
A year's age on me.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
I beseech you, sir,
Harm not yourself with your vexation
I am senseless of your wrath; a touch more rare
Subdues all pangs, all fears.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text: Past grace? Obedience?

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: Past hope, and in despair; that way, past grace.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text: That mightst have had the sole son of my queen!

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
O blest, that I might not! I chose an eagle,
And did avoid a puttock.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text:
Thou took'st a beggar; wouldst have made my throne
A seat for baseness.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
No; I rather added
A lustre to it.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text: O thou vile one!

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Sir,
It is your fault that I have loved Posthumus:
You bred him as my playfellow, and he is
A man worth any woman, overbuys me
Almost the sum he pays.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text: What, art thou mad?

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Almost, sir: heaven restore me! Would I were
A neat-herd's daughter, and my Leonatus
Our neighbour shepherd's son!

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text: Thou foolish thing!
Re-enter QUEEN

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text:
They were again together: you have done
Not after our command. Away with her,
And pen her up.

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
Beseech your patience. Peace,
Dear lady daughter, peace! Sweet sovereign,
Leave us to ourselves; and make yourself some comfort
Out of your best advice.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text:
Nay, let her languish
A drop of blood a day; and, being aged,
Die of this folly!
Exeunt CYMBELINE and Lords

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text: Fie! you must give way.
Enter PISANIO

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text: Here is your servant. How now, sir! What news?

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text: My lord your son drew on my master.

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
Ha!
No harm, I trust, is done?

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text:
There might have been,
But that my master rather play'd than fought
And had no help of anger: they were parted
By gentlemen at hand.

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text: I am very glad on't.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Your son's my father's friend; he takes his part.
To draw upon an exile! O brave sir!
I would they were in Afric both together;
Myself by with a needle, that I might prick
The goer-back. Why came you from your master?

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text:
On his command: he would not suffer me
To bring him to the haven; left these notes
Of what commands I should be subject to,
When 't pleased you to employ me.

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
This hath been
Your faithful servant: I dare lay mine honour
He will remain so.

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text: I humbly thank your highness.

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text: Pray, walk awhile.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
About some half-hour hence,
I pray you, speak with me: you shall at least
Go see my lord aboard: for this time leave me.
Exeunt
Enter CLOTEN and two Lords

units.speaker: FIRST LORD
units.text:
Sir, I would advise you to shift a shirt; the
violence of action hath made you reek as a
sacrifice: where air comes out, air comes in:
there's none abroad so wholesome as that you vent.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
If my shirt were bloody, then to shift it. Have I hurt
him?

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text: [Aside] No, 'faith; not so much as his patience.

units.speaker: FIRST LORD
units.text:
Hurt him! his body's a passable carcass, if he be
not hurt: it is a thoroughfare for steel, if it be not
hurt.

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text:
[Aside] His steel was in debt; it went o' the
backside the town.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: The villain would not stand me.

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text:
[Aside] No; but he fled forward still, toward your
face.

units.speaker: FIRST LORD
units.text:
Stand you! You have land enough of your own: but
he added to your having; gave you some ground.

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text: [Aside] As many inches as you have oceans. Puppies!

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: I would they had not come between us.

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text:
[Aside] So would I, till you had measured how long
a fool you were upon the ground.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: And that she should love this fellow and refuse me!

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text:
[Aside] If it be a sin to make a true election, she
is damned.

units.speaker: FIRST LORD
units.text:
Sir, as I told you always, her beauty and her brain
go not together: she's a good sign, but I have seen
small reflection of her wit.

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text:
[Aside] She shines not upon fools, lest the
reflection should hurt her.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
Come, I'll to my chamber. Would there had been some
hurt done!

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text:
[Aside] I wish not so; unless it had been the fall
of an ass, which is no great hurt.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: You'll go with us?

units.speaker: FIRST LORD
units.text: I'll attend your lordship.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: Nay, come, let's go together.

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text: Well, my lord.
Exeunt
Enter IMOGEN and PISANIO

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
I would thou grew'st unto the shores o' the haven,
And question'dst every sail: if he should write
And not have it, 'twere a paper lost,
As offer'd mercy is. What was the last
That he spake to thee?

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text: It was his queen, his queen!

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: Then waved his handkerchief?

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text: And kiss'd it, madam.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Senseless Linen! happier therein than I!
And that was all?

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text:
No, madam; for so long
As he could make me with this eye or ear
Distinguish him from others, he did keep
The deck, with glove, or hat, or handkerchief,
Still waving, as the fits and stirs of 's mind
Could best express how slow his soul sail'd on,
How swift his ship.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Thou shouldst have made him
As little as a crow, or less, ere left
To after-eye him.

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text: Madam, so I did.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
I would have broke mine eye-strings; crack'd them, but
To look upon him, till the diminution
Of space had pointed him sharp as my needle,
Nay, follow'd him, till he had melted from
The smallness of a gnat to air, and then
Have turn'd mine eye and wept. But, good Pisanio,
When shall we hear from him?

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text:
Be assured, madam,
With his next vantage.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
I did not take my leave of him, but had
Most pretty things to say: ere I could tell him
How I would think on him at certain hours
Such thoughts and such, or I could make him swear
The shes of Italy should not betray
Mine interest and his honour, or have charged him,
At the sixth hour of morn, at noon, at midnight,
To encounter me with orisons, for then
I am in heaven for him; or ere I could
Give him that parting kiss which I had set
Betwixt two charming words, comes in my father
And like the tyrannous breathing of the north
Shakes all our buds from growing.
Enter a Lady

units.speaker: LADY
units.text:
The queen, madam,
Desires your highness' company.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Those things I bid you do, get them dispatch'd.
I will attend the queen.

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text: Madam, I shall.
Exeunt
Enter PHILARIO, IACHIMO, a Frenchman, a Dutchman, and a Spaniard

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Believe it, sir, I have seen him in Britain: he was
then of a crescent note, expected to prove so worthy
as since he hath been allowed the name of; but I
could then have looked on him without the help of
admiration, though the catalogue of his endowments
had been tabled by his side and I to peruse him by
items.

units.speaker: PHILARIO
units.text:
You speak of him when he was less furnished than now
he is with that which makes him both without and
within.

units.speaker: FRENCHMAN
units.text:
I have seen him in France: we had very many there
could behold the sun with as firm eyes as he.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
This matter of marrying his king's daughter, wherein
he must be weighed rather by her value than his own,
words him, I doubt not, a great deal from the matter.

units.speaker: FRENCHMAN
units.text: And then his banishment.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Ay, and the approbation of those that weep this
lamentable divorce under her colours are wonderfully
to extend him; be it but to fortify her judgment,
which else an easy battery might lay flat, for
taking a beggar without less quality. But how comes
it he is to sojourn with you? How creeps
acquaintance?

units.speaker: PHILARIO
units.text:
His father and I were soldiers together; to whom I
have been often bound for no less than my life.
Here comes the Briton: let him be so entertained
amongst you as suits, with gentlemen of your
knowing, to a stranger of his quality.
Enter POSTHUMUS LEONATUS

units.speaker: PHILARIO
units.text:
I beseech you all, be better known to this
gentleman; whom I commend to you as a noble friend
of mine: how worthy he is I will leave to appear
hereafter, rather than story him in his own hearing.

units.speaker: FRENCHMAN
units.text: Sir, we have known together in Orleans.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Since when I have been debtor to you for courtesies,
which I will be ever to pay and yet pay still.

units.speaker: FRENCHMAN
units.text:
Sir, you o'er-rate my poor kindness: I was glad I
did atone my countryman and you; it had been pity
you should have been put together with so mortal a
purpose as then each bore, upon importance of so
slight and trivial a nature.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
By your pardon, sir, I was then a young traveller;
rather shunned to go even with what I heard than in
my every action to be guided by others' experiences:
but upon my mended judgment—if I offend not to say
it is mended—my quarrel was not altogether slight.

units.speaker: FRENCHMAN
units.text:
'Faith, yes, to be put to the arbitrement of swords,
and by such two that would by all likelihood have
confounded one the other, or have fallen both.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: Can we, with manners, ask what was the difference?

units.speaker: FRENCHMAN
units.text:
Safely, I think: 'twas a contention in public,
which may, without contradiction, suffer the report.
It was much like an argument that fell out last
night, where each of us fell in praise of our
country mistresses; this gentleman at that time
vouching—and upon warrant of bloody
affirmation—his to be more fair, virtuous, wise,
chaste, constant-qualified and less attemptable
than any the rarest of our ladies in France.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
That lady is not now living, or this gentleman's
opinion by this worn out.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text: She holds her virtue still and I my mind.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: You must not so far prefer her 'fore ours of Italy.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Being so far provoked as I was in France, I would
abate her nothing, though I profess myself her
adorer, not her friend.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
As fair and as good—a kind of hand-in-hand
comparison—had been something too fair and too good
for any lady in Britain. If she went before others
I have seen, as that diamond of yours outlustres
many I have beheld. I could not but believe she
excelled many: but I have not seen the most
precious diamond that is, nor you the lady.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text: I praised her as I rated her: so do I my stone.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: What do you esteem it at?

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text: More than the world enjoys.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Either your unparagoned mistress is dead, or she's
outprized by a trifle.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
You are mistaken: the one may be sold, or given, if
there were wealth enough for the purchase, or merit
for the gift: the other is not a thing for sale,
and only the gift of the gods.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: Which the gods have given you?

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text: Which, by their graces, I will keep.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
You may wear her in title yours: but, you know,
strange fowl light upon neighbouring ponds. Your
ring may be stolen too: so your brace of unprizable
estimations; the one is but frail and the other
casual; a cunning thief, or a that way accomplished
courtier, would hazard the winning both of first and
last.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Your Italy contains none so accomplished a courtier
to convince the honour of my mistress, if, in the
holding or loss of that, you term her frail. I do
nothing doubt you have store of thieves;
notwithstanding, I fear not my ring.

units.speaker: PHILARIO
units.text: Let us leave here, gentlemen.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Sir, with all my heart. This worthy signior, I
thank him, makes no stranger of me; we are familiar at
first.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
With five times so much conversation, I should get
ground of your fair mistress, make her go back, even
to the yielding, had I admittance and opportunity to
friend.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text: No, no.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
I dare thereupon pawn the moiety of my estate to
your ring; which, in my opinion, o'ervalues it
something: but I make my wager rather against your
confidence than her reputation: and, to bar your
offence herein too, I durst attempt it against any
lady in the world.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
You are a great deal abused in too bold a
persuasion; and I doubt not you sustain what you're
worthy of by your attempt.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: What's that?

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
A repulse: though your attempt, as you call it,
deserve more; a punishment too.

units.speaker: PHILARIO
IACHIMO
units.text:
Would I had put my estate and my neighbour's on the
approbation of what I have spoke!

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text: What lady would you choose to assail?

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Yours; whom in constancy you think stands so safe.
I will lay you ten thousand ducats to your ring,
that, commend me to the court where your lady is,
with no more advantage than the opportunity of a
second conference, and I will bring from thence
that honour of hers which you imagine so reserved.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
I will wage against your gold, gold to it: my ring
I hold dear as my finger; 'tis part of it.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
You are afraid, and therein the wiser. If you buy
ladies' flesh at a million a dram, you cannot
preserve it from tainting: but I see you have some
religion in you, that you fear.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
This is but a custom in your tongue; you bear a
graver purpose, I hope.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
I am the master of my speeches, and would undergo
what's spoken, I swear.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Will you? I shall but lend my diamond till your
return: let there be covenants drawn between's: my
mistress exceeds in goodness the hugeness of your
unworthy thinking: I dare you to this match: here's my
ring.

units.speaker: PHILARIO
units.text: I will have it no lay.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
By the gods, it is one. If I bring you no
sufficient testimony that I have enjoyed the dearest
bodily part of your mistress, my ten thousand ducats
are yours; so is your diamond too: if I come off,
and leave her in such honour as you have trust in,
she your jewel, this your jewel, and my gold are
yours: provided I have your commendation for my more
free entertainment.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
I embrace these conditions; let us have articles
betwixt us. Only, thus far you shall answer: if
you make your voyage upon her and give me directly
to understand you have prevailed, I am no further
your enemy; she is not worth our debate: if she
remain unseduced, you not making it appear
otherwise, for your ill opinion and the assault you
have made to her chastity you shall answer me with
your sword.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Your hand; a covenant: we will have these things set
down by lawful counsel, and straight away for
Britain, lest the bargain should catch cold and
starve: I will fetch my gold and have our two
wagers recorded.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text: Agreed.
Exeunt POSTHUMUS LEONATUS and IACHIMO

units.speaker: FRENCHMAN
units.text: Will this hold, think you?

units.speaker: PHILARIO
units.text:
Signior Iachimo will not from it.
Pray, let us follow 'em.
Exeunt
Enter QUEEN, Ladies, and CORNELIUS

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
Whiles yet the dew's on ground, gather those flowers;
Make haste: who has the note of them?

units.speaker: FIRST LADY
units.text: I, madam.

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text: Dispatch.
Exeunt Ladies

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text: Now, master doctor, have you brought those drugs?

units.speaker: CORNELIUS
units.text: Pleaseth your highness, ay: here they are, madam:

units.speaker: CORNELIUS
units.text:
But I beseech your grace, without offence,—
My conscience bids me ask— wherefore you have
Commanded of me those most poisonous compounds,
Which are the movers of a languishing death;
But though slow, deadly?

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
I wonder, doctor,
Thou ask'st me such a question. Have I not been
Thy pupil long? Hast thou not learn'd me how
To make perfumes? distil? preserve? yea, so
That our great king himself doth woo me oft
For my confections? Having thus far proceeded,—
Unless thou think'st me devilish—is't not meet
That I did amplify my judgment in
Other conclusions? I will try the forces
Of these thy compounds on such creatures as
We count not worth the hanging, but none human,
To try the vigour of them and apply
Allayments to their act, and by them gather
Their several virtues and effects.

units.speaker: CORNELIUS
units.text:
Your highness
Shall from this practise but make hard your heart:
Besides, the seeing these effects will be
Both noisome and infectious.

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text: O, content thee.
Enter PISANIO
Aside

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
Here comes a flattering rascal; upon him
Will I first work: he's for his master,
An enemy to my son. How now, Pisanio!
Doctor, your service for this time is ended;
Take your own way.

units.speaker: CORNELIUS
units.text:
[Aside] I do suspect you, madam;
But you shall do no harm.

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text: [To PISANIO] Hark thee, a word.

units.speaker: CORNELIUS
units.text:
[Aside] I do not like her. She doth think she has
Strange lingering poisons: I do know her spirit,
And will not trust one of her malice with
A drug of such damn'd nature. Those she has
Will stupefy and dull the sense awhile;
Which first, perchance, she'll prove on
cats and dogs,
Then afterward up higher: but there is
No danger in what show of death it makes,
More than the locking-up the spirits a time,
To be more fresh, reviving. She is fool'd
With a most false effect; and I the truer,
So to be false with her.

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
No further service, doctor,
Until I send for thee.

units.speaker: CORNELIUS
units.text: I humbly take my leave.
Exit

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
Weeps she still, say'st thou? Dost thou think in time
She will not quench and let instructions enter
Where folly now possesses? Do thou work:
When thou shalt bring me word she loves my son,
I'll tell thee on the instant thou art then
As great as is thy master, greater, for
His fortunes all lie speechless and his name
Is at last gasp: return he cannot, nor
Continue where he is: to shift his being
Is to exchange one misery with another,
And every day that comes comes to decay
A day's work in him. What shalt thou expect,
To be depender on a thing that leans,
Who cannot be new built, nor has no friends,
So much as but to prop him?

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
Thou takest up
Thou know'st not what; but take it for thy labour:
It is a thing I made, which hath the king
Five times redeem'd from death: I do not know
What is more cordial. Nay, I prethee, take it;
It is an earnest of a further good
That I mean to thee. Tell thy mistress how
The case stands with her; do't as from thyself.
Think what a chance thou changest on, but think
Thou hast thy mistress still, to boot, my son,
Who shall take notice of thee: I'll move the king
To any shape of thy preferment such
As thou'lt desire; and then myself, I chiefly,
That set thee on to this desert, am bound
To load thy merit richly. Call my women:
Think on my words.
Exit PISANIO

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
A sly and constant knave,
Not to be shaked; the agent for his master
And the remembrancer of her to hold
The hand-fast to her lord. I have given him that
Which, if he take, shall quite unpeople her
Of liegers for her sweet, and which she after,
Except she bend her humour, shall be assured
To taste of too.
Re-enter PISANIO and Ladies

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
So, so: well done, well done:
The violets, cowslips, and the primroses,
Bear to my closet. Fare thee well, Pisanio;
Think on my words.
Exeunt QUEEN and Ladies

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text:
And shall do:
But when to my good lord I prove untrue,
I'll choke myself: there's all I'll do for you.
Exit
Enter IMOGEN

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
A father cruel, and a step-dame false;
A foolish suitor to a wedded lady,
That hath her husband banish'd;—O, that husband!
My supreme crown of grief! and those repeated
Vexations of it! Had I been thief-stol'n,
As my two brothers, happy! but most miserable
Is the desire that's glorious: blest be those,
How mean soe'er, that have their honest wills,
Which seasons comfort. Who may this be? Fie!
Enter PISANIO and IACHIMO

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text:
Madam, a noble gentleman of Rome,
Comes from my lord with letters.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Change you, madam?
The worthy Leonatus is in safety
And greets your highness dearly.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Thanks, good sir:
You're kindly welcome.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
[Aside] All of her that is out of door most rich!
If she be furnish'd with a mind so rare,
She is alone the Arabian bird, and I
Have lost the wager. Boldness be my friend!
Arm me, audacity, from head to foot!
Or, like the Parthian, I shall flying fight;
Rather directly fly.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
[Reads] 'He is one of the noblest note, to whose
kindnesses I am most infinitely tied. Reflect upon
him accordingly, as you value your trust—
LEONATUS.'
So far I read aloud:
But even the very middle of my heart
Is warm'd by the rest, and takes it thankfully.
You are as welcome, worthy sir, as I
Have words to bid you, and shall find it so
In all that I can do.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Thanks, fairest lady.
What, are men mad? Hath nature given them eyes
To see this vaulted arch, and the rich crop
Of sea and land, which can distinguish 'twixt
The fiery orbs above and the twinn'd stones
Upon the number'd beach? and can we not
Partition make with spectacles so precious
'Twixt fair and foul?

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: What makes your admiration?

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
It cannot be i' the eye, for apes and monkeys
'Twixt two such shes would chatter this way and
Contemn with mows the other; nor i' the judgment,
For idiots in this case of favour would
Be wisely definite; nor i' the appetite;
Sluttery to such neat excellence opposed
Should make desire vomit emptiness,
Not so allured to feed.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: What is the matter, trow?

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
The cloyed will,
That satiate yet unsatisfied desire, that tub
Both fill'd and running, ravening first the lamb
Longs after for the garbage.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
What, dear sir,
Thus raps you? Are you well?

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: Thanks, madam; well.

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text:
I was going, sir,
To give him welcome.
Exit

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: Continues well my lord? His health, beseech you?

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: Well, madam.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: Is he disposed to mirth? I hope he is.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Exceeding pleasant; none a stranger there
So merry and so gamesome: he is call'd
The Briton reveller.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
When he was here,
He did incline to sadness, and oft-times
Not knowing why.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
I never saw him sad.
There is a Frenchman his companion, one
An eminent monsieur, that, it seems, much loves
A Gallian girl at home; he furnaces
The thick sighs from him, whiles the jolly Briton—
Your lord, I mean—laughs from's free lungs, cries 'O,
Can my sides hold, to think that man, who knows
By history, report, or his own proof,
What woman is, yea, what she cannot choose
But must be, will his free hours languish for
Assured bondage?'

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: Will my lord say so?

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Ay, madam, with his eyes in flood with laughter:
It is a recreation to be by
And hear him mock the Frenchman. But, heavens know,
Some men are much to blame.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: Not he, I hope.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Not he: but yet heaven's bounty towards him might
Be used more thankfully. In himself, 'tis much;
In you, which I account his beyond all talents,
Whilst I am bound to wonder, I am bound
To pity too.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: What do you pity, sir?

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: Two creatures heartily.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Am I one, sir?
You look on me: what wreck discern you in me
Deserves your pity?

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Lamentable! What,
To hide me from the radiant sun and solace
I' the dungeon by a snuff?

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
I pray you, sir,
Deliver with more openness your answers
To my demands. Why do you pity me?

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
That others do—
I was about to say—enjoy your— But
It is an office of the gods to venge it,
Not mine to speak on 't.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
You do seem to know
Something of me, or what concerns me: pray you,—
Since doubting things go ill often hurts more
Than to be sure they do; for certainties
Either are past remedies, or, timely knowing,
The remedy then born—discover to me
What both you spur and stop.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Had I this cheek
To bathe my lips upon; this hand, whose touch,
Whose every touch, would force the feeler's soul
To the oath of loyalty; this object, which
Takes prisoner the wild motion of mine eye,
Fixing it only here; should I, damn'd then,
Slaver with lips as common as the stairs
That mount the Capitol; join gripes with hands
Made hard with hourly falsehood—falsehood, as
With labour; then by-peeping in an eye
Base and unlustrous as the smoky light
That's fed with stinking tallow; it were fit
That all the plagues of hell should at one time
Encounter such revolt.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
My lord, I fear,
Has forgot Britain.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
And himself. Not I,
Inclined to this intelligence, pronounce
The beggary of his change; but 'tis your graces
That from my mutest conscience to my tongue
Charms this report out.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: Let me hear no more.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
O dearest soul! your cause doth strike my heart
With pity, that doth make me sick. A lady
So fair, and fasten'd to an empery,
Would make the great'st king double,— to be partner'd
With tomboys hired with that self-exhibition
Which your own coffers yield! with diseased ventures
That play with all infirmities for gold
Which rottenness can lend nature! such boil'd stuff
As well might poison poison! Be revenged;
Or she that bore you was no queen, and you
Recoil from your great stock.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Revenged!
How should I be revenged? If this be true,—
As I have such a heart that both mine ears
Must not in haste abuse—if it be true,
How should I be revenged?

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Should he make me
Live, like Diana's priest, betwixt cold sheets,
Whiles he is vaulting variable ramps,
In your despite, upon your purse? Revenge it.
I dedicate myself to your sweet pleasure,
More noble than that runagate to your bed,
And will continue fast to your affection,
Still close as sure.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: What, ho, Pisanio!

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: Let me my service tender on your lips.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Away! I do condemn mine ears that have
So long attended thee. If thou wert honourable,
Thou wouldst have told this tale for virtue, not
For such an end thou seek'st,—as base as strange.
Thou wrong'st a gentleman, who is as far
From thy report as thou from honour, and
Solicit'st here a lady that disdains
Thee and the devil alike. What ho, Pisanio!
The king my father shall be made acquainted
Of thy assault: if he shall think it fit,
A saucy stranger in his court to mart
As in a Romish stew and to expound
His beastly mind to us, he hath a court
He little cares for and a daughter who
He not respects at all. What, ho, Pisanio!

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
O happy Leonatus! I may say
The credit that thy lady hath of thee
Deserves thy trust, and thy most perfect goodness
Her assured credit. Blessed live you long!
A lady to the worthiest sir that ever
Country call'd his! and you his mistress, only
For the most worthiest fit! Give me your pardon.
I have spoke this, to know if your affiance
Were deeply rooted; and shall make your lord,
That which he is, new o'er: and he is one
The truest manner'd; such a holy witch
That he enchants societies into him;
Half all men's hearts are his.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: You make amends.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
He sits 'mongst men like a descended god:
He hath a kind of honour sets him off,
More than a mortal seeming. Be not angry,
Most mighty princess, that I have adventured
To try your taking a false report; which hath
Honour'd with confirmation your great judgment
In the election of a sir so rare,
Which you know cannot err: the love I bear him
Made me to fan you thus, but the gods made you,
Unlike all others, chaffless. Pray, your pardon.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
All's well, sir: take my power i' the court
for yours.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
My humble thanks. I had almost forgot
To entreat your grace but in a small request,
And yet of moment to, for it concerns
Your lord; myself and other noble friends,
Are partners in the business.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: Pray, what is't?

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Some dozen Romans of us and your lord—
The best feather of our wing—have mingled sums
To buy a present for the emperor
Which I, the factor for the rest, have done
In France: 'tis plate of rare device, and jewels
Of rich and exquisite form; their values great;
And I am something curious, being strange,
To have them in safe stowage: may it please you
To take them in protection?

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Willingly;
And pawn mine honour for their safety: since
My lord hath interest in them, I will keep them
In my bedchamber.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
They are in a trunk,
Attended by my men: I will make bold
To send them to you, only for this night;
I must aboard to-morrow.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: O, no, no.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Yes, I beseech; or I shall short my word
By lengthening my return. From Gallia
I cross'd the seas on purpose and on promise
To see your grace.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
I thank you for your pains:
But not away to-morrow!

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
O, I must, madam:
Therefore I shall beseech you, if you please
To greet your lord with writing, do't to-night:
I have outstood my time; which is material
To the tender of our present.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
I will write.
Send your trunk to me; it shall safe be kept,
And truly yielded you. You're very welcome.
Exeunt
Enter CLOTEN and two Lords

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
Was there ever man had such luck! when I kissed the
jack, upon an up-cast to be hit away! I had a
hundred pound on't: and then a whoreson jackanapes
must take me up for swearing; as if I borrowed mine
oaths of him and might not spend them at my pleasure.

units.speaker: FIRST LORD
units.text:
What got he by that? You have broke his pate with
your bowl.

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text:
[Aside] If his wit had been like him that broke it,
it would have run all out.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
When a gentleman is disposed to swear, it is not for
any standers-by to curtail his oaths, ha?

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text: No my lord;

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
Whoreson dog! I give him satisfaction?
Would he had been one of my rank!

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text: [Aside] To have smelt like a fool.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
I am not vexed more at any thing in the earth: a
pox on't! I had rather not be so noble as I am;
they dare not fight with me, because of the queen my
mother: every Jack-slave hath his bellyful of
fighting, and I must go up and down like a cock that
nobody can match.

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text:
[Aside] You are cock and capon too; and you crow,
cock, with your comb on.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: Sayest thou?

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text:
It is not fit your lordship should undertake every
companion that you give offence to.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
No, I know that: but it is fit I should commit
offence to my inferiors.

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text: Ay, it is fit for your lordship only.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: Why, so I say.

units.speaker: FIRST LORD
units.text:
Did you hear of a stranger that's come to court
to-night?

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: A stranger, and I not know on't!

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text:
[Aside] He's a strange fellow himself, and knows it
not.

units.speaker: FIRST LORD
units.text:
There's an Italian come; and, 'tis thought, one of
Leonatus' friends.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
Leonatus! a banished rascal; and he's another,
whatsoever he be. Who told you of this stranger?

units.speaker: FIRST LORD
units.text: One of your lordship's pages.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
Is it fit I went to look upon him? is there no
derogation in't?

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text: You cannot derogate, my lord.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: Not easily, I think.

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text:
[Aside] You are a fool granted; therefore your
issues, being foolish, do not derogate.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
Come, I'll go see this Italian: what I have lost
to-day at bowls I'll win to-night of him. Come, go.

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text: I'll attend your lordship.
Exeunt CLOTEN and First Lord

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text:
That such a crafty devil as is his mother
Should yield the world this ass! a woman that
Bears all down with her brain; and this her son
Cannot take two from twenty, for his heart,
And leave eighteen. Alas, poor princess,
Thou divine Imogen, what thou endurest,
Betwixt a father by thy step-dame govern'd,
A mother hourly coining plots, a wooer
More hateful than the foul expulsion is
Of thy dear husband, than that horrid act
Of the divorce he'ld make! The heavens hold firm
The walls of thy dear honour, keep unshaked
That temple, thy fair mind, that thou mayst stand,
To enjoy thy banish'd lord and this great land!
Exit
A trunk is carried in. IMOGEN in bed, reading; a Lady attending

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: Who's there? my woman Helen?

units.speaker: LADY
units.text: Please you, madam

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: What hour is it?

units.speaker: LADY
units.text: Almost midnight, madam.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
I have read three hours then: mine eyes are weak:
Fold down the leaf where I have left: to bed:
Take not away the taper, leave it burning;
And if thou canst awake by four o' the clock,
I prithee, call me. Sleep hath seized me wholly
Exit Lady

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
To your protection I commend me, gods.
From fairies and the tempters of the night
Guard me, beseech ye.
Sleeps. IACHIMO comes from the trunk

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
The crickets sing, and man's o'er-labour'd sense
Repairs itself by rest. Our Tarquin thus
Did softly press the rushes, ere he waken'd
The chastity he wounded. Cytherea,
How bravely thou becomest thy bed, fresh lily,
And whiter than the sheets! That I might touch!
But kiss; one kiss! Rubies unparagon'd,
How dearly they do't! 'Tis her breathing that
Perfumes the chamber thus: the flame o' the taper
Bows toward her, and would under-peep her lids,
To see the enclosed lights, now canopied
Under these windows, white and azure laced
With blue of heaven's own tinct. But my design,
To note the chamber: I will write all down:
Such and such pictures; there the window; such
The adornment of her bed; the arras; figures,
Why, such and such; and the contents o' the story.
Ah, but some natural notes about her body,
Above ten thousand meaner moveables
Would testify, to enrich mine inventory.
O sleep, thou ape of death, lie dull upon her!
And be her sense but as a monument,
Thus in a chapel lying! Come off, come off:
Clock strikes

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: One, two, three: time, time!
An ante-chamber adjoining Imogen's apartments. Enter CLOTEN and Lords.

units.speaker: FIRST LORD
units.text:
Your lordship is the most patient man in loss, the
most coldest that ever turned up ace.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: It would make any man cold to lose.

units.speaker: FIRST LORD
units.text:
But not every man patient after the noble temper of
your lordship. You are most hot and furious when you
win.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
Winning will put any man into courage. If I could
get this foolish Imogen, I should have gold enough.
It's almost morning, is't not?

units.speaker: FIRST LORD
units.text: Day, my lord.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
I would this music would come: I am advised to give
her music o' mornings; they say it will penetrate.
Enter Musicians

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
Come on; tune: if you can penetrate her with your
fingering, so; we'll try with tongue too: if none
will do, let her remain; but I'll never give o'er.
First, a very excellent good-conceited thing;
after, a wonderful sweet air, with admirable rich
words to it: and then let her consider.
SONG

units.speaker: MUSICIANS
units.text:
Hark, hark! the lark at heaven's gate sings,
And Phoebus 'gins arise,
His steeds to water at those springs
On chaliced flowers that lies;
And winking Mary-buds begin
To ope their golden eyes:
With every thing that pretty is,
My lady sweet, arise:
Arise, arise.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
So, get you gone. If this penetrate, I will
consider your music the better: if it do not, it is
a vice in her ears, which horse-hairs and
calves'-guts, nor the voice of unpaved eunuch to
boot, can never amend.
Exeunt Musicians

units.speaker: SECOND LORD
units.text: Here comes the king.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
I am glad I was up so late; for that's the reason I
was up so early: he cannot choose but take this
service I have done fatherly.
Enter CYMBELINE and QUEEN

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: Good morrow to your majesty and to my gracious mother.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text:
Attend you here the door of our stern daughter?
Will she not forth?

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
I have assailed her with music, but she vouchsafes no
notice.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text:
The exile of her minion is too new;
She hath not yet forgot him: some more time
Must wear the print of his remembrance out,
And then she's yours.

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
You are most bound to the king,
Who lets go by no vantages that may
Prefer you to his daughter. Frame yourself
To orderly soliciting, and be friended
With aptness of the season; make denials
Increase your services; so seem as if
You were inspired to do those duties which
You tender to her; that you in all obey her,
Save when command to your dismission tends,
And therein you are senseless.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: Senseless! not so.
Enter a Messenger

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text:
So like you, sir, ambassadors from Rome;
The one is Caius Lucius.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text:
A worthy fellow,
Albeit he comes on angry purpose now;
But that's no fault of his: we must receive him
According to the honour of his sender;
And towards himself, his goodness forespent on us,
We must extend our notice. Our dear son,
When you have given good morning to your mistress,
Attend the queen and us; we shall have need
To employ you towards this Roman. Come, our queen.
Exeunt all but CLOTEN

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
If she be up, I'll speak with her; if not,
Let her lie still and dream.
Enter a Lady

units.speaker: LADY
units.text: Who's there that knocks?

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: A gentleman.

units.speaker: LADY
units.text: No more?

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: Yes, and a gentlewoman's son.

units.speaker: LADY
units.text:
That's more
Than some, whose tailors are as dear as yours,
Can justly boast of. What's your lordship's pleasure?

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: Your lady's person: is she ready?

units.speaker: LADY
units.text:
Ay,
To keep her chamber.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
There is gold for you;
Sell me your good report.

units.speaker: LADY
units.text:
How! my good name? or to report of you
What I shall think is good?—The princess!
Enter IMOGEN

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: Good morrow, fairest: sister, your sweet hand.
Exit Lady

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Good morrow, sir. You lay out too much pains
For purchasing but trouble; the thanks I give
Is telling you that I am poor of thanks
And scarce can spare them.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: Still, I swear I love you.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
If you but said so, 'twere as deep with me:
If you swear still, your recompense is still
That I regard it not.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: This is no answer.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
But that you shall not say I yield being silent,
I would not speak. I pray you, spare me: 'faith,
I shall unfold equal discourtesy
To your best kindness: one of your great knowing
Should learn, being taught, forbearance.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
To leave you in your madness, 'twere my sin:
I will not.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: Fools are not mad folks.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: Do you call me fool?

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
As I am mad, I do:
If you'll be patient, I'll no more be mad;
That cures us both. I am much sorry, sir,
You put me to forget a lady's manners,
By being so verbal: and learn now, for all,
That I, which know my heart, do here pronounce,
By the very truth of it, I care not for you,
And am so near the lack of charity—
To accuse myself—I hate you; which I had rather
You felt than make't my boast.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
You sin against
Obedience, which you owe your father. For
The contract you pretend with that base wretch,
One bred of alms and foster'd with cold dishes,
With scraps o' the court, it is no contract, none:
And though it be allow'd in meaner parties—
Yet who than he more mean?—to knit their souls,
On whom there is no more dependency
But brats and beggary, in self-figured knot;
Yet you are curb'd from that enlargement by
The consequence o' the crown, and must not soil
The precious note of it with a base slave.
A hilding for a livery, a squire's cloth,
A pantler, not so eminent.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Profane fellow
Wert thou the son of Jupiter and no more
But what thou art besides, thou wert too base
To be his groom: thou wert dignified enough,
Even to the point of envy, if 'twere made
Comparative for your virtues, to be styled
The under-hangman of his kingdom, and hated
For being preferred so well.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: The south-fog rot him!

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
He never can meet more mischance than come
To be but named of thee. His meanest garment,
That ever hath but clipp'd his body, is dearer
In my respect than all the hairs above thee,
Were they all made such men. How now, Pisanio!
Enter PISANIO

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: 'His garment!' Now the devil—

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: To Dorothy my woman hie thee presently—

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: 'His garment!'

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
I am sprited with a fool.
Frighted, and anger'd worse: go bid my woman
Search for a jewel that too casually
Hath left mine arm: it was thy master's: 'shrew me,
If I would lose it for a revenue
Of any king's in Europe. I do think
I saw't this morning: confident I am
Last night 'twas on mine arm; I kiss'd it:
I hope it be not gone to tell my lord
That I kiss aught but he.

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text: 'Twill not be lost.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: I hope so: go and search.
Exit PISANIO

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
You have abused me:
'His meanest garment!'

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Ay, I said so, sir:
If you will make't an action, call witness to't.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text: I will inform your father.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Your mother too:
She's my good lady, and will conceive, I hope,
But the worst of me. So, I leave you, sir,
To the worst of discontent.
Exit

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
I'll be revenged:
'His meanest garment!' Well.
Exit
Enter POSTHUMUS and PHILARIO

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Fear it not, sir: I would I were so sure
To win the king as I am bold her honour
Will remain hers.

units.speaker: PHILARIO
units.text: What means do you make to him?

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Not any, but abide the change of time,
Quake in the present winter's state and wish
That warmer days would come: in these sear'd hopes,
I barely gratify your love; they failing,
I must die much your debtor.

units.speaker: PHILARIO
units.text:
Your very goodness and your company
O'erpays all I can do. By this, your king
Hath heard of great Augustus: Caius Lucius
Will do's commission throughly: and I think
He'll grant the tribute, send the arrearages,
Or look upon our Romans, whose remembrance
Is yet fresh in their grief.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
I do believe,
Statist though I am none, nor like to be,
That this will prove a war; and you shall hear
The legions now in Gallia sooner landed
In our not-fearing Britain than have tidings
Of any penny tribute paid. Our countrymen
Are men more order'd than when Julius Caesar
Smiled at their lack of skill, but found
their courage
Worthy his frowning at: their discipline,
Now mingled with their courages, will make known
To their approvers they are people such
That mend upon the world.
Enter IACHIMO

units.speaker: PHILARIO
units.text: See! Iachimo!

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
PHILARIO
units.text: Welcome, sir.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
I hope the briefness of your answer made
The speediness of your return.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Your lady
Is one of the fairest that I have look'd upon.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
And therewithal the best; or let her beauty
Look through a casement to allure false hearts
And be false with them.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: Here are letters for you.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text: Their tenor good, I trust.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: 'Tis very like.

units.speaker: PHILARIO
units.text:
Was Caius Lucius in the Britain court
When you were there?

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
He was expected then,
But not approach'd.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
All is well yet.
Sparkles this stone as it was wont? or is't not
Too dull for your good wearing?

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
If I had lost it,
I should have lost the worth of it in gold.
I'll make a journey twice as far, to enjoy
A second night of such sweet shortness which
Was mine in Britain, for the ring is won.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text: The stone's too hard to come by.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Not a whit,
Your lady being so easy.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Make not, sir,
Your loss your sport: I hope you know that we
Must not continue friends.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Good sir, we must,
If you keep covenant. Had I not brought
The knowledge of your mistress home, I grant
We were to question further: but I now
Profess myself the winner of her honour,
Together with your ring; and not the wronger
Of her or you, having proceeded but
By both your wills.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
If you can make't apparent
That you have tasted her in bed, my hand
And ring is yours; if not, the foul opinion
You had of her pure honour gains or loses
Your sword or mine, or masterless leaves both
To who shall find them.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Sir, my circumstances,
Being so near the truth as I will make them,
Must first induce you to believe: whose strength
I will confirm with oath; which, I doubt not,
You'll give me leave to spare, when you shall find
You need it not.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text: Proceed.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
First, her bedchamber,—
Where, I confess, I slept not, but profess
Had that was well worth watching—it was hang'd
With tapesty of silk and silver; the story
Proud Cleopatra, when she met her Roman,
And Cydnus swell'd above the banks, or for
The press of boats or pride: a piece of work
So bravely done, so rich, that it did strive
In workmanship and value; which I wonder'd
Could be so rarely and exactly wrought,
Since the true life on't was—

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
IACHIMO
units.text:
More particulars
Must justify my knowledge.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
So they must,
Or do your honour injury.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
The chimney
Is south the chamber, and the chimney-piece
Chaste Dian bathing: never saw I figures
So likely to report themselves: the cutter
Was as another nature, dumb; outwent her,
Motion and breath left out.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
This is a thing
Which you might from relation likewise reap,
Being, as it is, much spoke of.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
The roof o' the chamber
With golden cherubins is fretted: her andirons—
I had forgot them—were two winking Cupids
Of silver, each on one foot standing, nicely
Depending on their brands.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
This is her honour!
Let it be granted you have seen all this—and praise
Be given to your remembrance— the description
Of what is in her chamber nothing saves
The wager you have laid.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: Then, if you can,

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Jove!
Once more let me behold it: is it that
Which I left with her?

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
Sir—I thank her—that:
She stripp'd it from her arm; I see her yet;
Her pretty action did outsell her gift,
And yet enrich'd it too: she gave it me, and said
She prized it once.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
May be she pluck'd it off
To send it me.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: She writes so to you, doth she?

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text: O, no, no, no! 'tis true. Here, take this too;

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
It is a basilisk unto mine eye,
Kills me to look on't. Let there be no honour
Where there is beauty; truth, where semblance; love,
Where there's another man: the vows of women
Of no more bondage be, to where they are made,
Than they are to their virtues; which is nothing.
O, above measure false!

units.speaker: PHILARIO
units.text:
Have patience, sir,
And take your ring again; 'tis not yet won:
It may be probable she lost it; or
Who knows if one of her women, being corrupted,
Hath stol'n it from her?

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Very true;
And so, I hope, he came by't. Back my ring:
Render to me some corporal sign about her,
More evident than this; for this was stolen.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: By Jupiter, I had it from her arm.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Hark you, he swears; by Jupiter he swears.
'Tis true:—nay, keep the ring—'tis true: I am sure
She would not lose it: her attendants are
All sworn and honourable:—they induced to steal it!
And by a stranger!—No, he hath enjoyed her:
The cognizance of her incontinency
Is this: she hath bought the name of whore
thus dearly.
There, take thy hire; and all the fiends of hell
Divide themselves between you!

units.speaker: PHILARIO
units.text:
Sir, be patient:
This is not strong enough to be believed
Of one persuaded well of—

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Never talk on't;
She hath been colted by him.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text:
If you seek
For further satisfying, under her breast—
Worthy the pressing—lies a mole, right proud
Of that most delicate lodging: by my life,
I kiss'd it; and it gave me present hunger
To feed again, though full. You do remember
This stain upon her?

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Ay, and it doth confirm
Another stain, as big as hell can hold,
Were there no more but it.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: Will you hear more?

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Spare your arithmetic: never count the turns;
Once, and a million!

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: I'll be sworn—

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
No swearing.
If you will swear you have not done't, you lie;
And I will kill thee, if thou dost deny
Thou'st made me cuckold.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: I'll deny nothing.

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
O, that I had her here, to tear her limb-meal!
I will go there and do't, i' the court, before
Her father. I'll do something—
Exit

units.speaker: PHILARIO
units.text:
Quite besides
The government of patience! You have won:
Let's follow him, and pervert the present wrath
He hath against himself.

units.speaker: IACHIMO
units.text: With all my heart.
Exeunt
Enter POSTHUMUS LEONATUS

units.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
units.text:
Is there no way for men to be but women
Must be half-workers? We are all bastards;
And that most venerable man which I
Did call my father, was I know not where
When I was stamp'd; some coiner with his tools
Made me a counterfeit: yet my mother seem'd
The Dian of that time so doth my wife
The nonpareil of this. O, vengeance, vengeance!
Me of my lawful pleasure she restrain'd
And pray'd me oft forbearance; did it with
A prudency so rosy the sweet view on't
Might well have warm'd old Saturn; that I thought her
As chaste as unsunn'd snow. O, all the devils!
This yellow Iachimo, in an hour,—wast not?—
Or less,—at first?—perchance he spoke not, but,
Like a full-acorn'd boar, a German one,
Cried 'O!' and mounted; found no opposition
But what he look'd for should oppose and she
Should from encounter guard. Could I find out
The woman's part in me! For there's no motion
That tends to vice in man, but I affirm
It is the woman's part: be it lying, note it,
The woman's; flattering, hers; deceiving, hers;
Lust and rank thoughts, hers, hers; revenges, hers;
Ambitions, covetings, change of prides, disdain,
Nice longing, slanders, mutability,
All faults that may be named, nay, that hell knows,
Why, hers, in part or all; but rather, all;
For even to vice
They are not constant but are changing still
One vice, but of a minute old, for one
Not half so old as that. I'll write against them,
Detest them, curse them: yet 'tis greater skill
In a true hate, to pray they have their will:
The very devils cannot plague them better.
Exit
Enter in state, CYMBELINE, QUEEN, CLOTEN, and Lords at one door, and at another, CAIUS LUCIUS and Attendants

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text: Now say, what would Augustus Caesar with us?

units.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
units.text:
When Julius Caesar, whose remembrance yet
Lives in men's eyes and will to ears and tongues
Be theme and hearing ever, was in this Britain
And conquer'd it, Cassibelan, thine uncle,—
Famous in Caesar's praises, no whit less
Than in his feats deserving it—for him
And his succession granted Rome a tribute,
Yearly three thousand pounds, which by thee lately
Is left untender'd.

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
And, to kill the marvel,
Shall be so ever.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
There be many Caesars,
Ere such another Julius. Britain is
A world by itself; and we will nothing pay
For wearing our own noses.

units.speaker: QUEEN
units.text:
That opportunity
Which then they had to take from 's, to resume
We have again. Remember, sir, my liege,
The kings your ancestors, together with
The natural bravery of your isle, which stands
As Neptune's park, ribbed and paled in
With rocks unscalable and roaring waters,
With sands that will not bear your enemies' boats,
But suck them up to the topmast. A kind of conquest
Caesar made here; but made not here his brag
Of 'Came' and 'saw' and 'overcame: ' with shame—
That first that ever touch'd him—he was carried
From off our coast, twice beaten; and his shipping—
Poor ignorant baubles!— upon our terrible seas,
Like egg-shells moved upon their surges, crack'd
As easily 'gainst our rocks: for joy whereof
The famed Cassibelan, who was once at point—
O giglot fortune!—to master Caesar's sword,
Made Lud's town with rejoicing fires bright
And Britons strut with courage.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
Come, there's no more tribute to be paid: our
kingdom is stronger than it was at that time; and,
as I said, there is no moe such Caesars: other of
them may have crook'd noses, but to owe such
straight arms, none.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text: Son, let your mother end.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
We have yet many among us can gripe as hard as
Cassibelan: I do not say I am one; but I have a
hand. Why tribute? why should we pay tribute? If
Caesar can hide the sun from us with a blanket, or
put the moon in his pocket, we will pay him tribute
for light; else, sir, no more tribute, pray you now.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text:
You must know,
Till the injurious Romans did extort
This tribute from us, we were free:
Caesar's ambition,
Which swell'd so much that it did almost stretch
The sides o' the world, against all colour here
Did put the yoke upon 's; which to shake off
Becomes a warlike people, whom we reckon
Ourselves to be.

units.speaker: LORDS
units.text: We do.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text:
Say, then, to Caesar,
Our ancestor was that Mulmutius which
Ordain'd our laws, whose use the sword of Caesar
Hath too much mangled; whose repair and franchise
Shall, by the power we hold, be our good deed,
Though Rome be therefore angry: Mulmutius made our
laws,
Who was the first of Britain which did put
His brows within a golden crown and call'd
Himself a king.

units.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
units.text:
I am sorry, Cymbeline,
That I am to pronounce Augustus Caesar—
Caesar, that hath more kings his servants than
Thyself domestic officers—thine enemy:
Receive it from me, then: war and confusion
In Caesar's name pronounce I 'gainst thee: look
For fury not to be resisted. Thus defied,
I thank thee for myself.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text:
Thou art welcome, Caius.
Thy Caesar knighted me; my youth I spent
Much under him; of him I gather'd honour;
Which he to seek of me again, perforce,
Behoves me keep at utterance. I am perfect
That the Pannonians and Dalmatians for
Their liberties are now in arms; a precedent
Which not to read would show the Britons cold:
So Caesar shall not find them.

units.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
units.text: Let proof speak.

units.speaker: CLOTEN
units.text:
His majesty bids you welcome. Make
pastime with us a day or two, or longer: if
you seek us afterwards in other terms, you
shall find us in our salt-water girdle: if you
beat us out of it, it is yours; if you fall in
the adventure, our crows shall fare the better
for you; and there's an end.

units.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
units.text: So, sir.

units.speaker: CYMBELINE
units.text:
I know your master's pleasure and he mine:
All the remain is 'Welcome!'
Exeunt
Enter PISANIO, with a letter

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text:
How? of adultery? Wherefore write you not
What monster's her accuser? Leonatus,
O master! what a strange infection
Is fall'n into thy ear! What false Italian,
As poisonous-tongued as handed, hath prevail'd
On thy too ready hearing? Disloyal! No:
She's punish'd for her truth, and undergoes,
More goddess-like than wife-like, such assaults
As would take in some virtue. O my master!
Thy mind to her is now as low as were
Thy fortunes. How! that I should murder her?
Upon the love and truth and vows which I
Have made to thy command? I, her? her blood?
If it be so to do good service, never
Let me be counted serviceable. How look I,
That I should seem to lack humanity
so much as this fact comes to?
Enter IMOGEN

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text: How now, Pisanio!

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text: Madam, here is a letter from my lord.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Who? thy lord? that is my lord, Leonatus!
O, learn'd indeed were that astronomer
That knew the stars as I his characters;
He'ld lay the future open. You good gods,
Let what is here contain'd relish of love,
Of my lord's health, of his content, yet not
That we two are asunder; let that grieve him:
Some griefs are med'cinable; that is one of them,
For it doth physic love: of his content,
All but in that! Good wax, thy leave. Blest be
You bees that make these locks of counsel! Lovers
And men in dangerous bonds pray not alike:
Though forfeiters you cast in prison, yet
You clasp young Cupid's tables. Good news, gods!

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text:
One score 'twixt sun and sun,
Madam, 's enough for you:

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Why, one that rode to's execution, man,
Could never go so slow: I have heard of
riding wagers,
Where horses have been nimbler than the sands
That run i' the clock's behalf. But this is foolery:
Go bid my woman feign a sickness; say
She'll home to her father: and provide me presently
A riding-suit, no costlier than would fit
A franklin's housewife.

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text: Madam, you're best consider.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
I see before me, man: nor here, nor here,
Nor what ensues, but have a fog in them,
That I cannot look through. Away, I prithee;
Do as I bid thee: there's no more to say,
Accessible is none but Milford way.
Exeunt
Enter, from the cave, BELARIUS; GUIDERIUS, and ARVIRAGUS following

units.speaker: BELARIUS
units.text:
A goodly day not to keep house, with such
Whose roof's as low as ours! Stoop, boys; this gate
Instructs you how to adore the heavens and bows you
To a morning's holy office: the gates of monarchs
Are arch'd so high that giants may jet through
And keep their impious turbans on, without
Good morrow to the sun. Hail, thou fair heaven!
We house i' the rock, yet use thee not so hardly
As prouder livers do.

units.speaker: GUIDERIUS
units.text: Hail, heaven!

units.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
units.text: Hail, heaven!

units.speaker: BELARIUS
units.text:
Now for our mountain sport: up to yond hill;
Your legs are young; I'll tread these flats. Consider,
When you above perceive me like a crow,
That it is place which lessens and sets off;
And you may then revolve what tales I have told you
Of courts, of princes, of the tricks in war:
This service is not service, so being done,
But being so allow'd: to apprehend thus,
Draws us a profit from all things we see;
And often, to our comfort, shall we find
The sharded beetle in a safer hold
Than is the full-wing'd eagle. O, this life
Is nobler than attending for a cheque,
Richer than doing nothing for a bauble,
Prouder than rustling in unpaid-for silk:
Such gain the cap of him that makes 'em fine,
Yet keeps his book uncross'd: no life to ours.

units.speaker: GUIDERIUS
units.text:
Out of your proof you speak: we, poor unfledged,
Have never wing'd from view o' the nest, nor know not
What air's from home. Haply this life is best,
If quiet life be best; sweeter to you
That have a sharper known; well corresponding
With your stiff age: but unto us it is
A cell of ignorance; travelling a-bed;
A prison for a debtor, that not dares
To stride a limit.

units.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
units.text:
What should we speak of
When we are old as you? when we shall hear
The rain and wind beat dark December, how,
In this our pinching cave, shall we discourse
The freezing hours away? We have seen nothing;
We are beastly, subtle as the fox for prey,
Like warlike as the wolf for what we eat;
Our valour is to chase what flies; our cage
We make a quire, as doth the prison'd bird,
And sing our bondage freely.

units.speaker: BELARIUS
units.text:
How you speak!
Did you but know the city's usuries
And felt them knowingly; the art o' the court
As hard to leave as keep; whose top to climb
Is certain falling, or so slippery that
The fear's as bad as falling; the toil o' the war,
A pain that only seems to seek out danger
I' the name of fame and honour; which dies i'
the search,
And hath as oft a slanderous epitaph
As record of fair act; nay, many times,
Doth ill deserve by doing well; what's worse,
Must court'sy at the censure:— O boys, this story
The world may read in me: my body's mark'd
With Roman swords, and my report was once
First with the best of note: Cymbeline loved me,
And when a soldier was the theme, my name
Was not far off: then was I as a tree
Whose boughs did bend with fruit: but in one night,
A storm or robbery, call it what you will,
Shook down my mellow hangings, nay, my leaves,
And left me bare to weather.

units.speaker: GUIDERIUS
units.text: Uncertain favour!

units.speaker: BELARIUS
units.text:
My fault being nothing—as I have told you oft—
But that two villains, whose false oaths prevail'd
Before my perfect honour, swore to Cymbeline
I was confederate with the Romans: so
Follow'd my banishment, and this twenty years
This rock and these demesnes have been my world;
Where I have lived at honest freedom, paid
More pious debts to heaven than in all
The fore-end of my time. But up to the mountains!
This is not hunters' language: he that strikes
The venison first shall be the lord o' the feast;
To him the other two shall minister;
And we will fear no poison, which attends
In place of greater state. I'll meet you in the
valleys.
Exeunt GUIDERIUS and ARVIRAGUS

units.speaker: BELARIUS
units.text:
How hard it is to hide the sparks of nature!
These boys know little they are sons to the king;
Nor Cymbeline dreams that they are alive.
They think they are mine; and though train'd
up thus meanly
I' the cave wherein they bow, their thoughts do hit
The roofs of palaces, and nature prompts them
In simple and low things to prince it much
Beyond the trick of others. This Polydore,
The heir of Cymbeline and Britain, who
The king his father call'd Guiderius,— Jove!
When on my three-foot stool I sit and tell
The warlike feats I have done, his spirits fly out
Into my story: say 'Thus, mine enemy fell,
And thus I set my foot on 's neck;' even then
The princely blood flows in his cheek, he sweats,
Strains his young nerves and puts himself in posture
That acts my words. The younger brother, Cadwal,
Once Arviragus, in as like a figure,
Strikes life into my speech and shows much more
His own conceiving.— Hark, the game is roused!
O Cymbeline! heaven and my conscience knows
Thou didst unjustly banish me: whereon,
At three and two years old, I stole these babes;
Thinking to bar thee of succession, as
Thou reft'st me of my lands. Euriphile,
Thou wast their nurse; they took thee for
their mother,
And every day do honour to her grave:
Myself, Belarius, that am Morgan call'd,
They take for natural father. The game is up.
Exit
Enter PISANIO and IMOGEN

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Thou told'st me, when we came from horse, the place
Was near at hand: ne'er long'd my mother so
To see me first, as I have now. Pisanio! man!
Where is Posthumus? What is in thy mind,
That makes thee stare thus? Wherefore breaks that sigh
From the inward of thee? One, but painted thus,
Would be interpreted a thing perplex'd
Beyond self-explication: put thyself
Into a havior of less fear, ere wildness
Vanquish my staider senses. What's the matter?
Why tender'st thou that paper to me, with
A look untender? If't be summer news,
Smile to't before; if winterly, thou need'st
But keep that countenance still. My husband's hand!
That drug-damn'd Italy hath out-craftied him,
And he's at some hard point. Speak, man: thy tongue
May take off some extremity, which to read
Would be even mortal to me.

units.speaker: PISANIO
IMOGEN
units.text:
[Reads] 'Thy mistress, Pisanio, hath played the
strumpet in my bed; the testimonies whereof lie
bleeding in me. I speak not out of weak surmises,
but from proof as strong as my grief and as certain
as I expect my revenge. That part thou, Pisanio,
must act for me, if thy faith be not tainted with
the breach of hers. Let thine own hands take away
her life: I shall give thee opportunity at
Milford-Haven. She hath my letter for the purpose
where, if thou fear to strike and to make me certain
it is done, thou art the pandar to her dishonour and
equally to me disloyal.'

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text:
What shall I need to draw my sword? the paper
Hath cut her throat already. No, 'tis slander,
Whose edge is sharper than the sword, whose tongue
Outvenoms all the worms of Nile, whose breath
Rides on the posting winds and doth belie
All corners of the world: kings, queens and states,
Maids, matrons, nay, the secrets of the grave
This viperous slander enters. What cheer, madam?

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
False to his bed! What is it to be false?
To lie in watch there and to think on him?
To weep 'twixt clock and clock? if sleep
charge nature,
To break it with a fearful dream of him
And cry myself awake? that's false to's bed, is it?

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text: Alas, good lady!

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
I false! Thy conscience witness: Iachimo,
Thou didst accuse him of incontinency;
Thou then look'dst like a villain; now methinks
Thy favour's good enough. Some jay of Italy
Whose mother was her painting, hath betray'd him:
Poor I am stale, a garment out of fashion;
And, for I am richer than to hang by the walls,
I must be ripp'd:—to pieces with me!— O,
Men's vows are women's traitors! All good seeming,
By thy revolt, O husband, shall be thought
Put on for villany; not born where't grows,
But worn a bait for ladies.

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text: Good madam, hear me.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
True honest men being heard, like false Aeneas,
Were in his time thought false, and Sinon's weeping
Did scandal many a holy tear, took pity
From most true wretchedness: so thou, Posthumus,
Wilt lay the leaven on all proper men;
Goodly and gallant shall be false and perjured
From thy great fall. Come, fellow, be thou honest:
Do thou thy master's bidding: when thou see'st him,
A little witness my obedience: look!
I draw the sword myself: take it, and hit
The innocent mansion of my love, my heart;
Fear not; 'tis empty of all things but grief;
Thy master is not there, who was indeed
The riches of it: do his bidding; strike
Thou mayst be valiant in a better cause;
But now thou seem'st a coward.

units.speaker: PISANIO
units.text:
Hence, vile instrument!
Thou shalt not damn my hand.

units.speaker: IMOGEN
units.text:
Why, I must die;
And if I do not by thy hand, thou art
No servant of thy master's. Against self-slaughter
There is a prohibition so divine
That cravens my weak hand. Come, here's my heart.
Something's afore't. Soft, soft! we'll no defence;
Obedient as the scabbard. What is here?
The scriptures of the loyal Leonatus,
All turn'd to heresy? Away, away,
Corrupters of my faith! you shall no more
Be stomachers to my heart. Thus may poor fools
Believe false teachers: though those that
are betray'd
Do feel the treason sharply, yet the traitor
Stands in worse case of woe.
And thou, Posthumus, thou that didst set up
