POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
IACHIMO
sentences.text:
More particulars
Must justify my knowledge.

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
So they must,
Or do your honour injury.

sentences.speaker: IACHIMO
sentences.text:
The chimney
Is south the chamber, and the chimney-piece
Chaste Dian bathing: never saw I figures
So likely to report themselves: the cutter
Was as another nature, dumb; outwent her,
Motion and breath left out.

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
This is a thing
Which you might from relation likewise reap,
Being, as it is, much spoke of.

sentences.speaker: IACHIMO
sentences.text:
The roof o' the chamber
With golden cherubins is fretted: her andirons—
I had forgot them—were two winking Cupids
Of silver, each on one foot standing, nicely
Depending on their brands.

sentences.speaker: IACHIMO
sentences.text:
The roof o' the chamber
With golden cherubins is fretted: her andirons—
I had forgot them—were two winking Cupids
Of silver, each on one foot standing, nicely
Depending on their brands.

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
This is her honour!
Let it be granted you have seen all this—and praise
Be given to your remembrance— the description
Of what is in her chamber nothing saves
The wager you have laid.

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
Jove!
Once more let me behold it: is it that
Which I left with her?

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
Jove!
Once more let me behold it: is it that
Which I left with her?

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
Jove!
Once more let me behold it: is it that
Which I left with her?

sentences.speaker: IACHIMO
sentences.text:
Sir—I thank her—that:
She stripp'd it from her arm; I see her yet;
Her pretty action did outsell her gift,
And yet enrich'd it too: she gave it me, and said
She prized it once.

May be she pluck'd it off
To send it me.

sentences.speaker: IACHIMO
sentences.text: She writes so to you, doth she?

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text: O, no, no, no! 'tis true. Here, take this too;

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text: O, no, no, no! 'tis true. Here, take this too;

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text: O, no, no, no! 'tis true. Here, take this too;

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text: O, no, no, no! 'tis true. Here, take this too;

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
It is a basilisk unto mine eye,
Kills me to look on't. Let there be no honour
Where there is beauty; truth, where semblance; love,
Where there's another man: the vows of women
Of no more bondage be, to where they are made,
Than they are to their virtues; which is nothing.
O, above measure false!

sentences.speaker: PHILARIO
sentences.text:
Have patience, sir,
And take your ring again; 'tis not yet won:
It may be probable she lost it; or
Who knows if one of her women, being corrupted,
Hath stol'n it from her?

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
Very true;
And so, I hope, he came by't. Back my ring:
Render to me some corporal sign about her,
More evident than this; for this was stolen.

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:

Very true;
And so, I hope, he came by't. Back my ring:
Render to me some corporal sign about her,
More evident than this; for this was stolen.

sentences.speaker: IACHIMO
sentences.text: By Jupiter, I had it from her arm.

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
Hark you, he swears; by Jupiter he swears.
'Tis true:—nay, keep the ring—'tis true: I am sure
She would not lose it: her attendants are
All sworn and honourable:—they induced to steal it!
And by a stranger!—No, he hath enjoyed her:
The cognizance of her incontinency
Is this: she hath bought the name of whore
thus dearly.
There, take thy hire; and all the fiends of hell
Divide themselves between you!

And by a stranger!—No, he hath enjoyed her:
The cognizance of her incontinency
Is this: she hath bought the name of whore
thus dearly.
There, take thy hire; and all the fiends of hell
Divide themselves between you!

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
Hark you, he swears; by Jupiter he swears.
'Tis true:—nay, keep the ring—'tis true: I am sure
She would not lose it: her attendants are
All sworn and honourable:—they induced to steal it!
And by a stranger!—No, he hath enjoyed her:
The cognizance of her incontinency
Is this: she hath bought the name of whore
thus dearly.
There, take thy hire; and all the fiends of hell
Divide themselves between you!

sentences.speaker: PHILARIO
sentences.text:
Sir, be patient:
This is not strong enough to be believed
Of one persuaded well of—

sentences.speaker: PHILARIO
sentences.text:
Sir, be patient:
This is not strong enough to be believed
Of one persuaded well of—

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
Never talk on't;
She hath been colted by him.

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
Never talk on't;
She hath been colted by him.

sentences.speaker: IACHIMO
sentences.text:
If you seek
For further satisfying, under her breast—
Worthy the pressing—lies a mole, right proud
Of that most delicate lodging: by my life,
I kiss'd it; and it gave me present hunger
To feed again, though full. You do remember
This stain upon her?

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
Ay, and it doth confirm
Another stain, as big as hell can hold,
Were there no more but it.

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
Ay, and it doth confirm
Another stain, as big as hell can hold,
Were there no more but it.

sentences.speaker: IACHIMO
sentences.text: Will you hear more?

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
Spare your arithmetic: never count the turns;
Once, and a million!

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
Spare your arithmetic: never count the turns;
Once, and a million!

sentences.speaker: IACHIMO
sentences.text: I'll be sworn—

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
No swearing.
If you will swear you have not done't, you lie;
And I will kill thee, if thou dost deny
Thou'st made me cuckold.

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
No swearing.
If you will swear you have not done't, you lie;
And I will kill thee, if thou dost deny
Thou'st made me cuckold.

sentences.text:
No swearing.
If you will swear you have not done't, you lie;
And I will kill thee, if thou dost deny
Thou'st made me cuckold.

sentences.speaker: IACHIMO
sentences.text: I'll deny nothing.

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
O, that I had her here, to tear her limb-meal!
I will go there and do't, i' the court, before
Her father. I'll do something—

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
O, that I had her here, to tear her limb-meal!

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
O, that I had her here, to tear her limb-meal!

sentences.speaker: PHILARIO
sentences.text:
Quite besides
The government of patience! You have won:
Let's follow him, and pervert the present wrath
He hath against himself.

sentences.speaker: PHILARIO
sentences.text:
Quite besides
The government of patience! You have won:
Let's follow him, and pervert the present wrath
He hath against himself.

sentences.speaker: PHILARIO
sentences.text:
Quite besides
The government of patience! You have won:
Let's follow him, and pervert the present wrath
He hath against himself.

sentences.speaker: IACHIMO
sentences.text: With all my heart.
Exeunt
Enter POSTHUMUS LEONATUS

sentences.speaker: POSTHUMUS LEONATUS
sentences.text:
Is there no way for men to be but women
Must be half-workers? We are all bastards;
And that most venerable man which I
Did call my father, was I know not where
When I was stamp'd; some coiner with his tools
Made me a counterfeit: yet my mother seem'd
The Dian of that time so doth my wife
The nonpareil of this. O, vengeance, vengeance!
Me of my lawful pleasure she restrain'd
And pray'd me oft forbearance; did it with
A prudency so rosy the sweet view on't
Might well have warm'd old Saturn; that I thought her
As chaste as unsunn'd snow. O, all the devils!
This yellow Iachimo, in an hour,—wast not?—
Or less,—at first?—perchance he spoke not, but,
Like a full-acorn'd boar, a German one,
Cried 'O!' and mounted; found no opposition
But what he look'd for should oppose and she
Should from encounter guard. Could I find out
The woman's part in me! For there's no motion
That tends to vice in man, but I affirm
It is the woman's part: be it lying, note it,
The woman's; flattering, hers; deceiving, hers;
Lust and rank thoughts, hers, hers; revenges, hers;
Ambitions, covetings, change of prides, disdain,
Nice longing, slanders, mutability,
All faults that may be named, nay, that hell knows,
Why, hers, in part or all; but rather, all;
For even to vice
They are not constant but are changing still
One vice, but of a minute old, for one
Not half so old as that. I'll write against them,
Detest them, curse them: yet 'tis greater skill
In a true hate, to pray they have their will:
The very devils cannot plague them better.

sentences.text:
Is there no way for men to be but women
Must be half-workers? We are all bastards;
And that most venerable man which I
Did call my father, was I know not where
When I was stamp'd; some coiner with his tools
Made me a counterfeit: yet my mother seem'd
The Dian of that time so doth my wife
The nonpareil of this. O, vengeance, vengeance!
Me of my lawful pleasure she restrain'd
And pray'd me oft forbearance; did it with
A prudency so rosy the sweet view on't
Might well have warm'd old Saturn; that I thought her
As chaste as unsunn'd snow. O, all the devils!
This yellow Iachimo, in an hour,—wast not?—
Or less,—at first?—perchance he spoke not, but,
Like a full-acorn'd boar, a German one,
Cried 'O!' and mounted; found no opposition
But what he look'd for should oppose and she
Should from encounter guard. Could I find out
The woman's part in me! For there's no motion
That tends to vice in man, but I affirm
It is the woman's part: be it lying, note it,
The woman's; flattering, hers; deceiving, hers;
Lust and rank thoughts, hers, hers; revenges, hers;
Ambitions, covetings, change of prides, disdain,
Nice longing, slanders, mutability,
All faults that may be named, nay, that hell knows,
Why, hers, in part or all; but rather, all;
For even to vice
They are not constant but are changing still
One vice, but of a minute old, for one
Not half so old as that. I'll write against them,
Detest them, curse them: yet 'tis greater skill
In a true hate, to pray they have their will:
The very devils cannot plague them better.

Enter in state, CYMBELINE, QUEEN, CLOTEN, and Lords at one door, and at another, CAIUS LUCIUS and Attendants
Enter in state, CYMBELINE, QUEEN, CLOTEN, and Lords at one door, and at another, CAIUS LUCIUS and Attendants

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text: Now say, what would Augustus Caesar with us?

sentences.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
sentences.text:
When Julius Caesar, whose remembrance yet
Lives in men's eyes and will to ears and tongues
Be theme and hearing ever, was in this Britain
And conquer'd it, Cassibelan, thine uncle,—
Famous in Caesar's praises, no whit less
Than in his feats deserving it—for him
And his succession granted Rome a tribute,
Yearly three thousand pounds, which by thee lately
Is left untender'd.

sentences.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
sentences.text:
When Julius Caesar, whose remembrance yet
Lives in men's eyes and will to ears and tongues
Be theme and hearing ever, was in this Britain
And conquer'd it, Cassibelan, thine uncle,—
Famous in Caesar's praises, no whit less
Than in his feats deserving it—for him
And his succession granted Rome a tribute,
Yearly three thousand pounds, which by thee lately
Is left untender'd.

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text:
And, to kill the marvel,
Shall be so ever.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
There be many Caesars,
Ere such another Julius. Britain is
A world by itself; and we will nothing pay
For wearing our own noses.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
There be many Caesars,
Ere such another Julius. Britain is
A world by itself; and we will nothing pay
For wearing our own noses.

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text:
That opportunity
Which then they had to take from 's, to resume
We have again. Remember, sir, my liege,
The kings your ancestors, together with
The natural bravery of your isle, which stands
As Neptune's park, ribbed and paled in
With rocks unscalable and roaring waters,
With sands that will not bear your enemies' boats,
But suck them up to the topmast. A kind of conquest
Caesar made here; but made not here his brag
Of 'Came' and 'saw' and 'overcame: ' with shame—
That first that ever touch'd him—he was carried
From off our coast, twice beaten; and his shipping—
Poor ignorant baubles!— upon our terrible seas,
Like egg-shells moved upon their surges, crack'd
As easily 'gainst our rocks: for joy whereof
The famed Cassibelan, who was once at point—
O giglot fortune!—to master Caesar's sword,
Made Lud's town with rejoicing fires bright
And Britons strut with courage.

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text:
That opportunity
Which then they had to take from 's, to resume
We have again. Remember, sir, my liege,
The kings your ancestors, together with
The natural bravery of your isle, which stands
As Neptune's park, ribbed and paled in
With rocks unscalable and roaring waters,
With sands that will not bear your enemies' boats,
But suck them up to the topmast. A kind of conquest
Caesar made here; but made not here his brag
Of 'Came' and 'saw' and 'overcame: ' with shame—
That first that ever touch'd him—he was carried
From off our coast, twice beaten; and his shipping—
Poor ignorant baubles!— upon our terrible seas,
Like egg-shells moved upon their surges, crack'd
As easily 'gainst our rocks: for joy whereof
The famed Cassibelan, who was once at point—
O giglot fortune!—to master Caesar's sword,
Made Lud's town with rejoicing fires bright
And Britons strut with courage.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Come, there's no more tribute to be paid: our
kingdom is stronger than it was at that time; and,
as I said, there is no moe such Caesars: other of
them may have crook'd noses, but to owe such
straight arms, none.

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text: Son, let your mother end.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
We have yet many among us can gripe as hard as
Cassibelan: I do not say I am one; but I have a
hand. Why tribute? why should we pay tribute? If
Caesar can hide the sun from us with a blanket, or
put the moon in his pocket, we will pay him tribute
for light; else, sir, no more tribute, pray you now.

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
You must know,
Till the injurious Romans did extort
This tribute from us, we were free:
Caesar's ambition,
Which swell'd so much that it did almost stretch
The sides o' the world, against all colour here
Did put the yoke upon 's; which to shake off
Becomes a warlike people, whom we reckon
Ourselves to be.

sentences.speaker: LORDS
sentences.text: We do.

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
Say, then, to Caesar,
Our ancestor was that Mulmutius which
Ordain'd our laws, whose use the sword of Caesar
Hath too much mangled; whose repair and franchise
Shall, by the power we hold, be our good deed,
Though Rome be therefore angry: Mulmutius made our
laws,
Who was the first of Britain which did put
His brows within a golden crown and call'd
Himself a king.

sentences.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
sentences.text:
I am sorry, Cymbeline,
That I am to pronounce Augustus Caesar—
Caesar, that hath more kings his servants than
Thyself domestic officers—thine enemy:
Receive it from me, then: war and confusion
In Caesar's name pronounce I 'gainst thee: look
For fury not to be resisted. Thus defied,
I thank thee for myself.

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
Thou art welcome, Caius.
Thy Caesar knighted me; my youth I spent
Much under him; of him I gather'd honour;
Which he to seek of me again, perforce,
Behoves me keep at utterance. I am perfect
That the Pannonians and Dalmatians for
Their liberties are now in arms; a precedent
Which not to read would show the Britons cold:
So Caesar shall not find them.

sentences.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
sentences.text: Let proof speak.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
His majesty bids you welcome. Make
pastime with us a day or two, or longer: if
you seek us afterwards in other terms, you
shall find us in our salt-water girdle: if you
beat us out of it, it is yours; if you fall in
the adventure, our crows shall fare the better
for you; and there's an end.

sentences.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
sentences.text: So, sir.

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
I know your master's pleasure and he mine:
All the remain is 'Welcome!'

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
I know your master's pleasure and he mine:
All the remain is 'Welcome!'
Exeunt
Enter PISANIO, with a letter

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
How? of adultery? Wherefore write you not
What monster's her accuser? Leonatus,
O master! what a strange infection
Is fall'n into thy ear! What false Italian,
As poisonous-tongued as handed, hath prevail'd
On thy too ready hearing? Disloyal! No:
She's punish'd for her truth, and undergoes,
More goddess-like than wife-like, such assaults
As would take in some virtue. O my master!
Thy mind to her is now as low as were
Thy fortunes. How! that I should murder her?
Upon the love and truth and vows which I
Have made to thy command? I, her? her blood?
If it be so to do good service, never
Let me be counted serviceable. How look I,
That I should seem to lack humanity
so much as this fact comes to?

Have made to thy command? I, her? her blood?
If it be so to do good service, never
Let me be counted serviceable. How look I,
That I should seem to lack humanity
so much as this fact comes to?

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
How? of adultery? Wherefore write you not
What monster's her accuser? Leonatus,
O master! what a strange infection
Is fall'n into thy ear! What false Italian,
As poisonous-tongued as handed, hath prevail'd
On thy too ready hearing? Disloyal! No:
She's punish'd for her truth, and undergoes,
More goddess-like than wife-like, such assaults
As would take in some virtue. O my master!
Thy mind to her is now as low as were
Thy fortunes. How! that I should murder her?
Upon the love and truth and vows which I
Have made to thy command? I, her? her blood?
If it be so to do good service, never
Let me be counted serviceable. How look I,
That I should seem to lack humanity
so much as this fact comes to?

Enter IMOGEN

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text: How now, Pisanio!

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text: Madam, here is a letter from my lord.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Who? thy lord? that is my lord, Leonatus!
O, learn'd indeed were that astronomer
That knew the stars as I his characters;
He'ld lay the future open. You good gods,
Let what is here contain'd relish of love,
Of my lord's health, of his content, yet not
That we two are asunder; let that grieve him:
Some griefs are med'cinable; that is one of them,
For it doth physic love: of his content,
All but in that! Good wax, thy leave. Blest be
You bees that make these locks of counsel! Lovers
And men in dangerous bonds pray not alike:
Though forfeiters you cast in prison, yet
You clasp young Cupid's tables. Good news, gods!

Though forfeiters you cast in prison, yet
You clasp young Cupid's tables. Good news, gods!

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Who? thy lord? that is my lord, Leonatus!
O, learn'd indeed were that astronomer
That knew the stars as I his characters;
He'ld lay the future open. You good gods,
Let what is here contain'd relish of love,
Of my lord's health, of his content, yet not
That we two are asunder; let that grieve him:
Some griefs are med'cinable; that is one of them,
For it doth physic love: of his content,
All but in that! Good wax, thy leave. Blest be
You bees that make these locks of counsel! Lovers
And men in dangerous bonds pray not alike:
Though forfeiters you cast in prison, yet
You clasp young Cupid's tables. Good news, gods!

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
One score 'twixt sun and sun,
Madam, 's enough for you:

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
One score 'twixt sun and sun,
Madam, 's enough for you:

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
One score 'twixt sun and sun,
Madam, 's enough for you:

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Why, one that rode to's execution, man,
Could never go so slow: I have heard of
riding wagers,
Where horses have been nimbler than the sands
That run i' the clock's behalf. But this is foolery:
Go bid my woman feign a sickness; say
She'll home to her father: and provide me presently
A riding-suit, no costlier than would fit
A franklin's housewife.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text: Madam, you're best consider.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
I see before me, man: nor here, nor here,
Nor what ensues, but have a fog in them,
That I cannot look through. Away, I prithee;
Do as I bid thee: there's no more to say,
Accessible is none but Milford way.

Enter, from the cave, BELARIUS; GUIDERIUS, and ARVIRAGUS following
Enter, from the cave, BELARIUS; GUIDERIUS, and ARVIRAGUS following

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
A goodly day not to keep house, with such
Whose roof's as low as ours! Stoop, boys; this gate
Instructs you how to adore the heavens and bows you
To a morning's holy office: the gates of monarchs
Are arch'd so high that giants may jet through
And keep their impious turbans on, without
Good morrow to the sun. Hail, thou fair heaven!
We house i' the rock, yet use thee not so hardly
As prouder livers do.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text: Hail, heaven!

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text: Hail, heaven!

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
Now for our mountain sport: up to yond hill;
Your legs are young; I'll tread these flats. Consider,
When you above perceive me like a crow,
That it is place which lessens and sets off;
And you may then revolve what tales I have told you
Of courts, of princes, of the tricks in war:
This service is not service, so being done,
But being so allow'd: to apprehend thus,
Draws us a profit from all things we see;
And often, to our comfort, shall we find
The sharded beetle in a safer hold
Than is the full-wing'd eagle. O, this life
Is nobler than attending for a cheque,
Richer than doing nothing for a bauble,
Prouder than rustling in unpaid-for silk:
Such gain the cap of him that makes 'em fine,
Yet keeps his book uncross'd: no life to ours.

Than is the full-wing'd eagle. O, this life
Is nobler than attending for a cheque,
Richer than doing nothing for a bauble,
Prouder than rustling in unpaid-for silk:
Such gain the cap of him that makes 'em fine,
Yet keeps his book uncross'd: no life to ours.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
Now for our mountain sport: up to yond hill;
Your legs are young; I'll tread these flats. Consider,
When you above perceive me like a crow,
That it is place which lessens and sets off;
And you may then revolve what tales I have told you
Of courts, of princes, of the tricks in war:
This service is not service, so being done,
But being so allow'd: to apprehend thus,
Draws us a profit from all things we see;
And often, to our comfort, shall we find
The sharded beetle in a safer hold
Than is the full-wing'd eagle. O, this life
Is nobler than attending for a cheque,
Richer than doing nothing for a bauble,

Such gain the cap of him that makes 'em fine,
Yet keeps his book uncross'd: no life to ours.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
Out of your proof you speak: we, poor unfledged,
Have never wing'd from view o' the nest, nor know not
What air's from home. Haply this life is best,
If quiet life be best; sweeter to you
That have a sharper known; well corresponding
With your stiff age: but unto us it is
A cell of ignorance; travelling a-bed;
A prison for a debtor, that not dares
To stride a limit.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
What should we speak of
When we are old as you? when we shall hear
The rain and wind beat dark December, how,
In this our pinching cave, shall we discourse
The freezing hours away? We have seen nothing;
We are beastly, subtle as the fox for prey,
Like warlike as the wolf for what we eat;
Our valour is to chase what flies; our cage
We make a quire, as doth the prison'd bird,
And sing our bondage freely.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
How you speak!
Did you but know the city's usuries
And felt them knowingly; the art o' the court
As hard to leave as keep; whose top to climb
Is certain falling, or so slippery that
The fear's as bad as falling; the toil o' the war,
A pain that only seems to seek out danger
I' the name of fame and honour; which dies i'
the search,
And hath as oft a slanderous epitaph
As record of fair act; nay, many times,
Doth ill deserve by doing well; what's worse,
Must court'sy at the censure:— O boys, this story
The world may read in me: my body's mark'd
With Roman swords, and my report was once
First with the best of note: Cymbeline loved me,
And when a soldier was the theme, my name
Was not far off: then was I as a tree
Whose boughs did bend with fruit: but in one night,
A storm or robbery, call it what you will,
Shook down my mellow hangings, nay, my leaves,
And left me bare to weather.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text: Uncertain favour!

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
My fault being nothing—as I have told you oft—
But that two villains, whose false oaths prevail'd
Before my perfect honour, swore to Cymbeline
I was confederate with the Romans: so
Follow'd my banishment, and this twenty years
This rock and these demesnes have been my world;
Where I have lived at honest freedom, paid
More pious debts to heaven than in all
The fore-end of my time. But up to the mountains!
This is not hunters' language: he that strikes
The venison first shall be the lord o' the feast;
To him the other two shall minister;
And we will fear no poison, which attends
In place of greater state. I'll meet you in the
valleys.

But that two villains, whose false oaths prevail'd
Before my perfect honour, swore to Cymbeline
I was confederate with the Romans: so
Follow'd my banishment, and this twenty years
This rock and these demesnes have been my world;
Where I have lived at honest freedom, paid
More pious debts to heaven than in all
The fore-end of my time. But up to the mountains!
This is not hunters' language: he that strikes
The venison first shall be the lord o' the feast;
To him the other two shall minister;
And we will fear no poison, which attends
In place of greater state. I'll meet you in the
valleys.
Exeunt GUIDERIUS and ARVIRAGUS

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
How hard it is to hide the sparks of nature!
These boys know little they are sons to the king;
Nor Cymbeline dreams that they are alive.
They think they are mine; and though train'd
up thus meanly
I' the cave wherein they bow, their thoughts do hit
The roofs of palaces, and nature prompts them
In simple and low things to prince it much
Beyond the trick of others. This Polydore,
The heir of Cymbeline and Britain, who
The king his father call'd Guiderius,— Jove!
When on my three-foot stool I sit and tell
The warlike feats I have done, his spirits fly out
Into my story: say 'Thus, mine enemy fell,
And thus I set my foot on 's neck;' even then
The princely blood flows in his cheek, he sweats,
Strains his young nerves and puts himself in posture
That acts my words. The younger brother, Cadwal,
Once Arviragus, in as like a figure,
Strikes life into my speech and shows much more
His own conceiving.— Hark, the game is roused!
O Cymbeline! heaven and my conscience knows
Thou didst unjustly banish me: whereon,
At three and two years old, I stole these babes;
Thinking to bar thee of succession, as
Thou reft'st me of my lands. Euriphile,
Thou wast their nurse; they took thee for
their mother,
And every day do honour to her grave:
Myself, Belarius, that am Morgan call'd,
They take for natural father. The game is up.

Enter PISANIO and IMOGEN

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Thou told'st me, when we came from horse, the place
Was near at hand: ne'er long'd my mother so
To see me first, as I have now. Pisanio! man!
Where is Posthumus? What is in thy mind,
That makes thee stare thus? Wherefore breaks that sigh
From the inward of thee? One, but painted thus,
Would be interpreted a thing perplex'd
Beyond self-explication: put thyself
Into a havior of less fear, ere wildness
Vanquish my staider senses. What's the matter?
Why tender'st thou that paper to me, with
A look untender? If't be summer news,
Smile to't before; if winterly, thou need'st
But keep that countenance still. My husband's hand!
That drug-damn'd Italy hath out-craftied him,
And he's at some hard point. Speak, man: thy tongue
May take off some extremity, which to read
Would be even mortal to me.

Beyond self-explication: put thyself
Into a havior of less fear, ere wildness
Vanquish my staider senses. What's the matter?
Why tender'st thou that paper to me, with
A look untender? If't be summer news,
Smile to't before; if winterly, thou need'st
But keep that countenance still. My husband's hand!
That drug-damn'd Italy hath out-craftied him,
And he's at some hard point. Speak, man: thy tongue
May take off some extremity, which to read
Would be even mortal to me.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
PISANIO
IMOGEN
sentences.text:
[Reads] 'Thy mistress, Pisanio, hath played the
strumpet in my bed; the testimonies whereof lie
bleeding in me. I speak not out of weak surmises,
but from proof as strong as my grief and as certain
as I expect my revenge. That part thou, Pisanio,
must act for me, if thy faith be not tainted with
the breach of hers. Let thine own hands take away
her life: I shall give thee opportunity at
Milford-Haven. She hath my letter for the purpose
where, if thou fear to strike and to make me certain
it is done, thou art the pandar to her dishonour and
equally to me disloyal.'

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
What shall I need to draw my sword? the paper
Hath cut her throat already. No, 'tis slander,
Whose edge is sharper than the sword, whose tongue
Outvenoms all the worms of Nile, whose breath
Rides on the posting winds and doth belie
All corners of the world: kings, queens and states,
Maids, matrons, nay, the secrets of the grave
This viperous slander enters. What cheer, madam?

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
False to his bed! What is it to be false?
To lie in watch there and to think on him?
To weep 'twixt clock and clock? if sleep
charge nature,
To break it with a fearful dream of him
And cry myself awake? that's false to's bed, is it?

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text: Alas, good lady!

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
I false! Thy conscience witness: Iachimo,
Thou didst accuse him of incontinency;
Thou then look'dst like a villain; now methinks
Thy favour's good enough. Some jay of Italy
Whose mother was her painting, hath betray'd him:
Poor I am stale, a garment out of fashion;
And, for I am richer than to hang by the walls,
I must be ripp'd:—to pieces with me!— O,
Men's vows are women's traitors! All good seeming,
By thy revolt, O husband, shall be thought
Put on for villany; not born where't grows,
But worn a bait for ladies.

I must be ripp'd:—to pieces with me!— O,
Men's vows are women's traitors! All good seeming,
By thy revolt, O husband, shall be thought
Put on for villany; not born where't grows,
But worn a bait for ladies.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
I false! Thy conscience witness: Iachimo,
Thou didst accuse him of incontinency;
Thou then look'dst like a villain; now methinks
Thy favour's good enough. Some jay of Italy
Whose mother was her painting, hath betray'd him:
Poor I am stale, a garment out of fashion;
And, for I am richer than to hang by the walls,
I must be ripp'd:—to pieces with me!— O,
Men's vows are women's traitors! All good seeming,
By thy revolt, O husband, shall be thought
Put on for villany; not born where't grows,
But worn a bait for ladies.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text: Good madam, hear me.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
True honest men being heard, like false Aeneas,
Were in his time thought false, and Sinon's weeping
Did scandal many a holy tear, took pity
From most true wretchedness: so thou, Posthumus,
Wilt lay the leaven on all proper men;
Goodly and gallant shall be false and perjured
From thy great fall. Come, fellow, be thou honest:
Do thou thy master's bidding: when thou see'st him,
A little witness my obedience: look!
I draw the sword myself: take it, and hit
The innocent mansion of my love, my heart;
Fear not; 'tis empty of all things but grief;
Thy master is not there, who was indeed
The riches of it: do his bidding; strike
Thou mayst be valiant in a better cause;
But now thou seem'st a coward.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
Hence, vile instrument!
Thou shalt not damn my hand.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
Hence, vile instrument!
Thou shalt not damn my hand.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Why, I must die;
And if I do not by thy hand, thou art
No servant of thy master's. Against self-slaughter
There is a prohibition so divine
That cravens my weak hand. Come, here's my heart.
Something's afore't. Soft, soft! we'll no defence;
Obedient as the scabbard. What is here?
The scriptures of the loyal Leonatus,
All turn'd to heresy? Away, away,
Corrupters of my faith! you shall no more
Be stomachers to my heart. Thus may poor fools
Believe false teachers: though those that
are betray'd
Do feel the treason sharply, yet the traitor
Stands in worse case of woe.
And thou, Posthumus, thou that didst set up
My disobedience 'gainst the king my father
And make me put into contempt the suits
Of princely fellows, shalt hereafter find
It is no act of common passage, but
A strain of rareness: and I grieve myself
To think, when thou shalt be disedged by her
That now thou tirest on, how thy memory
Will then be pang'd by me. Prithee, dispatch:
The lamb entreats the butcher: where's thy knife?
Thou art too slow to do thy master's bidding,
When I desire it too.

Something's afore't. Soft, soft! we'll no defence;
Obedient as the scabbard. What is here?
The scriptures of the loyal Leonatus,
All turn'd to heresy? Away, away,
Corrupters of my faith! you shall no more
Be stomachers to my heart. Thus may poor fools
Believe false teachers: though those that
are betray'd
Do feel the treason sharply, yet the traitor
Stands in worse case of woe.
And thou, Posthumus, thou that didst set up
My disobedience 'gainst the king my father
And make me put into contempt the suits
Of princely fellows, shalt hereafter find
It is no act of common passage, but
A strain of rareness: and I grieve myself
To think, when thou shalt be disedged by her
That now thou tirest on, how thy memory
Will then be pang'd by me. Prithee, dispatch:
The lamb entreats the butcher: where's thy knife?
Thou art too slow to do thy master's bidding,
When I desire it too.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
O gracious lady,
Since I received command to do this business
I have not slept one wink.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text: Do't, and to bed then.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text: I'll wake mine eye-balls blind first.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Wherefore then
Didst undertake it? Why hast thou abused
So many miles with a pretence? this place?
Mine action and thine own? our horses' labour?
The time inviting thee? the perturb'd court,
For my being absent? whereunto I never
Purpose return. Why hast thou gone so far,
To be unbent when thou hast ta'en thy stand,
The elected deer before thee?

sentences.text:
But to win time
To lose so bad employment; in the which
I have consider'd of a course. Good lady,
Hear me with patience.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Talk thy tongue weary; speak
I have heard I am a strumpet; and mine ear
Therein false struck, can take no greater wound,
Nor tent to bottom that. But speak.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
Then, madam,
I thought you would not back again.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Most like;
Bringing me here to kill me.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
Not so, neither:
But if I were as wise as honest, then
My purpose would prove well. It cannot be
But that my master is abused:
Some villain, ay, and singular in his art.
Hath done you both this cursed injury.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text: Some Roman courtezan.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
No, on my life.
I'll give but notice you are dead and send him
Some bloody sign of it; for 'tis commanded
I should do so: you shall be miss'd at court,
And that will well confirm it.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Why good fellow,
What shall I do the while? where bide? how live?
Or in my life what comfort, when I am
Dead to my husband?

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text: If you'll back to the court—

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
No court, no father; nor no more ado
With that harsh, noble, simple nothing,
That Cloten, whose love-suit hath been to me
As fearful as a siege.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
No court, no father; nor no more ado
With that harsh, noble, simple nothing,
That Cloten, whose love-suit hath been to me
As fearful as a siege.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
If not at court,
Then not in Britain must you bide.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Where then
Hath Britain all the sun that shines? Day, night,
Are they not but in Britain? I' the world's volume
Our Britain seems as of it, but not in 't;
In a great pool a swan's nest: prithee, think
There's livers out of Britain.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
I am most glad
You think of other place. The ambassador,
Lucius the Roman, comes to Milford-Haven
To-morrow: now, if you could wear a mind
Dark as your fortune is, and but disguise
That which, to appear itself, must not yet be
But by self-danger, you should tread a course
Pretty and full of view; yea, haply, near
The residence of Posthumus; so nigh at least
That though his actions were not visible, yet
Report should render him hourly to your ear
As truly as he moves.

O, for such means!
Though peril to my modesty, not death on't,
I would adventure.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
O, for such means!

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
Well, then, here's the point:
You must forget to be a woman; change
Command into obedience: fear and niceness—
The handmaids of all women, or, more truly,
Woman its pretty self—into a waggish courage:
Ready in gibes, quick-answer'd, saucy and
As quarrelous as the weasel; nay, you must
Forget that rarest treasure of your cheek,
Exposing it—but, O, the harder heart!
Alack, no remedy!—to the greedy touch
Of common-kissing Titan, and forget
Your laboursome and dainty trims, wherein
You made great Juno angry.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Nay, be brief
I see into thy end, and am almost
A man already.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Nay, be brief
I see into thy end, and am almost
A man already.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
First, make yourself but like one.
Fore-thinking this, I have already fit—
'Tis in my cloak-bag—doublet, hat, hose, all
That answer to them: would you in their serving,
And with what imitation you can borrow
From youth of such a season, 'fore noble Lucius
Present yourself, desire his service, tell him
wherein you're happy,—which you'll make him know,
If that his head have ear in music,—doubtless
With joy he will embrace you, for he's honourable
And doubling that, most holy. Your means abroad,
You have me, rich; and I will never fail
Beginning nor supplyment.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Thou art all the comfort
The gods will diet me with. Prithee, away:
There's more to be consider'd; but we'll even
All that good time will give us: this attempt
I am soldier to, and will abide it with
A prince's courage. Away, I prithee.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
Well, madam, we must take a short farewell,
Lest, being miss'd, I be suspected of
Your carriage from the court. My noble mistress,
Here is a box; I had it from the queen:
What's in't is precious; if you are sick at sea,
Or stomach-qualm'd at land, a dram of this
Will drive away distemper. To some shade,
And fit you to your manhood. May the gods
Direct you to the best!

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text: Amen: I thank thee.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text: Amen: I thank thee.
Exeunt, severally
Enter CYMBELINE, QUEEN, CLOTEN, LUCIUS, Lords, and Attendants

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text: Thus far; and so farewell.

sentences.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
sentences.text:
Thanks, royal sir.
My emperor hath wrote, I must from hence;
And am right sorry that I must report ye
My master's enemy.

sentences.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
sentences.text:
Thanks, royal sir.
My emperor hath wrote, I must from hence;
And am right sorry that I must report ye
My master's enemy.

sentences.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
sentences.text:
Thanks, royal sir.
My emperor hath wrote, I must from hence;
And am right sorry that I must report ye
My master's enemy.

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
Our subjects, sir,
Will not endure his yoke; and for ourself
To show less sovereignty than they, must needs
Appear unkinglike.

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
Our subjects, sir,
Will not endure his yoke; and for ourself
To show less sovereignty than they, must needs
Appear unkinglike.

sentences.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
sentences.text:
So, sir: I desire of you
A conduct over-land to Milford-Haven.
Madam, all joy befal your grace!

sentences.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
sentences.text:
So, sir: I desire of you
A conduct over-land to Milford-Haven.
Madam, all joy befal your grace!

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text: And you!

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
CYMBELINE
CYMBELINE
CAIUS LUCIUS
sentences.text: Your hand, my lord.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Receive it friendly; but from this time forth
I wear it as your enemy.

sentences.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
sentences.text:
Sir, the event
Is yet to name the winner: fare you well.

sentences.speaker: CAIUS LUCIUS
sentences.text:
Sir, the event
Is yet to name the winner: fare you well.

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
Leave not the worthy Lucius, good my lords,
Till he have cross'd the Severn. Happiness!

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
Leave not the worthy Lucius, good my lords,
Till he have cross'd the Severn. Happiness!
Exeunt LUCIUS and Lords

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text:
He goes hence frowning: but it honours us
That we have given him cause.

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text:
He goes hence frowning: but it honours us
That we have given him cause.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
'Tis all the better;
Your valiant Britons have their wishes in it.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
'Tis all the better;
Your valiant Britons have their wishes in it.

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
Lucius hath wrote already to the emperor
How it goes here. It fits us therefore ripely
Our chariots and our horsemen be in readiness:
The powers that he already hath in Gallia
Will soon be drawn to head, from whence he moves
His war for Britain.

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text:
'Tis not sleepy business;
But must be look'd to speedily and strongly.

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
Our expectation that it would be thus
Hath made us forward. But, my gentle queen,
Where is our daughter? She hath not appear'd
Before the Roman, nor to us hath tender'd
The duty of the day: she looks us like
A thing more made of malice than of duty:
We have noted it. Call her before us; for
We have been too slight in sufferance.

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text:
Royal sir,
Since the exile of Posthumus, most retired
Hath her life been; the cure whereof, my lord,
'Tis time must do. Beseech your majesty,
Forbear sharp speeches to her: she's a lady
So tender of rebukes that words are strokes
And strokes death to her.

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
Where is she, sir? How
Can her contempt be answer'd?

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
Where is she, sir? How
Can her contempt be answer'd?

sentences.speaker: ATTENDANT
sentences.text:
Please you, sir,
Her chambers are all lock'd; and there's no answer
That will be given to the loudest noise we make.

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text:
My lord, when last I went to visit her,
She pray'd me to excuse her keeping close,
Whereto constrain'd by her infirmity,
She should that duty leave unpaid to you,
Which daily she was bound to proffer: this
She wish'd me to make known; but our great court
Made me to blame in memory.

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
Her doors lock'd?
Not seen of late? Grant, heavens, that which I fear
Prove false!

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:
Her doors lock'd?
Not seen of late? Grant, heavens, that which I fear
Prove false!

sentences.speaker: CYMBELINE
sentences.text:

Not seen of late? Grant, heavens, that which I fear
Prove false!
Exit

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text: Son, I say, follow the king.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
That man of hers, Pisanio, her old servant,
have not seen these two days.

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text: Go, look after.
Exit CLOTEN

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text:
Pisanio, thou that stand'st so for Posthumus!
He hath a drug of mine; I pray his absence
Proceed by swallowing that, for he believes
It is a thing most precious. But for her,
Where is she gone? Haply, despair hath seized her,
Or, wing'd with fervor of her love, she's flown
To her desired Posthumus: gone she is
To death or to dishonour; and my end
Can make good use of either: she being down,
I have the placing of the British crown.

sentences.text:
Pisanio, thou that stand'st so for Posthumus!
He hath a drug of mine; I pray his absence
Proceed by swallowing that, for he believes
It is a thing most precious. But for her,
Where is she gone? Haply, despair hath seized her,
Or, wing'd with fervor of her love, she's flown
To her desired Posthumus: gone she is
To death or to dishonour; and my end
Can make good use of either: she being down,
I have the placing of the British crown.
Re-enter CLOTEN

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text: How now, my son!

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
'Tis certain she is fled.
Go in and cheer the king: he rages; none
Dare come about him.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text:
[Aside] All the better: may
This night forestall him of the coming day!

sentences.speaker: QUEEN
sentences.text:
[Aside] All the better: may
This night forestall him of the coming day!

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
I love and hate her: for she's fair and royal,
And that she hath all courtly parts more exquisite
Than lady, ladies, woman; from every one
The best she hath, and she, of all compounded,
Outsells them all; I love her therefore: but
Disdaining me and throwing favours on
The low Posthumus slanders so her judgment
That what's else rare is choked; and in that point
I will conclude to hate her, nay, indeed,
To be revenged upon her. For when fools Shall—

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Who is here? What, are you packing, sirrah?
Come hither: ah, you precious pander! Villain,
Where is thy lady? In a word; or else
Thou art straightway with the fiends.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text: O, good my lord!

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Where is thy lady? Or, by Jupiter,—
I will not ask again. Close villain,
I'll have this secret from thy heart, or rip
Thy heart to find it. Is she with Posthumus?
From whose so many weights of baseness cannot
A dram of worth be drawn.

How can she be with him? When was she missed?
He is in Rome.

sentences.text: O, my all-worthy lord!

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
All-worthy villain!
Discover where thy mistress is at once,
At the next word: no more of 'worthy lord!'
Speak, or thy silence on the instant is
Thy condemnation and thy death.

This paper is the history of my knowledge
Touching her flight.

Let's see't. I will pursue her
Even to Augustus' throne.

[Aside] Or this, or perish.
She's far enough; and what he learns by this
May prove his travel, not her danger.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text: Hum!

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
[Aside] I'll write to my lord she's dead. O Imogen,
Safe mayst thou wander, safe return again!

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
[Aside] I'll write to my lord she's dead. O Imogen,
Safe mayst thou wander, safe return again!

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text: Sirrah, is this letter true?

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text: Sir, as I think.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
It is Posthumus' hand; I know't. Sirrah, if thou
wouldst not be a villain, but do me true service,
undergo those employments wherein I should have
cause to use thee with a serious industry, that is,
what villany soe'er I bid thee do, to perform it
directly and truly, I would think thee an honest
man: thou shouldst neither want my means for thy
relief nor my voice for thy preferment.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text: Well, my good lord.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Wilt thou serve me? for since patiently and
constantly thou hast stuck to the bare fortune of
that beggar Posthumus, thou canst not, in the
course of gratitude, but be a diligent follower of
mine: wilt thou serve me?

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text: Sir, I will.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Give me thy hand; here's my purse. Hast any of thy
late master's garments in thy possession?

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Give me thy hand; here's my purse. Hast any of thy
late master's garments in thy possession?

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
I have, my lord, at my lodging, the same suit he
wore when he took leave of my lady and mistress.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
The first service thou dost me, fetch that suit
hither: let it be thy lint service; go.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
The first service thou dost me, fetch that suit
hither: let it be thy lint service; go.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text: I shall, my lord.
Exit

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Meet thee at Milford-Haven!—I forgot to ask him one
thing; I'll remember't anon:—even there, thou
villain Posthumus, will I kill thee. I would these
garments were come. She said upon a time—the
bitterness of it I now belch from my heart—that she
held the very garment of Posthumus in more respect
than my noble and natural person together with the
adornment of my qualities. With that suit upon my
back, will I ravish her: first kill him, and in her
eyes; there shall she see my valour, which will then
be a torment to her contempt. He on the ground, my
speech of insultment ended on his dead body, and
when my lust hath dined,—which, as I say, to vex
her I will execute in the clothes that she so
praised,—to the court I'll knock her back, foot
her home again. She hath despised me rejoicingly,
and I'll be merry in my revenge.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text: Be those the garments?

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text: Ay, my noble lord.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text: How long is't since she went to Milford-Haven?

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text: She can scarce be there yet.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Bring this apparel to my chamber; that is the second
thing that I have commanded thee: the third is,
that thou wilt be a voluntary mute to my design. Be
but duteous, and true preferment shall tender itself
to thee. My revenge is now at Milford: would I had
wings to follow it! Come, and be true.

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
Thou bid'st me to my loss: for true to thee
Were to prove false, which I will never be,
To him that is most true. To Milford go,
And find not her whom thou pursuest. Flow, flow,
You heavenly blessings, on her! This fool's speed
Be cross'd with slowness; labour be his meed!

sentences.speaker: PISANIO
sentences.text:
Thou bid'st me to my loss: for true to thee
Were to prove false, which I will never be,
To him that is most true. To Milford go,
And find not her whom thou pursuest. Flow, flow,
You heavenly blessings, on her! This fool's speed
Be cross'd with slowness; labour be his meed!

Enter IMOGEN, in boy's clothes

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
I see a man's life is a tedious one:
I have tired myself, and for two nights together
Have made the ground my bed. I should be sick,
But that my resolution helps me. Milford,
When from the mountain-top Pisanio show'd thee,
Thou wast within a ken: O Jove! I think
Foundations fly the wretched; such, I mean,
Where they should be relieved. Two beggars told me
I could not miss my way: will poor folks lie,
That have afflictions on them, knowing 'tis
A punishment or trial? Yes; no wonder,
When rich ones scarce tell true. To lapse in fulness
Is sorer than to lie for need, and falsehood
Is worse in kings than beggars. My dear lord!
Thou art one o' the false ones. Now I think on thee,
My hunger's gone; but even before, I was
At point to sink for food. But what is this?
Here is a path to't: 'tis some savage hold:
I were best not to call; I dare not call:
yet famine,
Ere clean it o'erthrow nature, makes it valiant,
Plenty and peace breeds cowards: hardness ever
Of hardiness is mother. Ho! who's here?
If any thing that's civil, speak; if savage,
Take or lend. Ho! No answer? Then I'll enter.
Best draw my sword: and if mine enemy
But fear the sword like me, he'll scarcely look on't.
Such a foe, good heavens!

At point to sink for food. But what is this?
Here is a path to't: 'tis some savage hold:
I were best not to call; I dare not call:
yet famine,
Ere clean it o'erthrow nature, makes it valiant,
Plenty and peace breeds cowards: hardness ever
Of hardiness is mother. Ho! who's here?
If any thing that's civil, speak; if savage,
Take or lend. Ho! No answer? Then I'll enter.
Best draw my sword: and if mine enemy
But fear the sword like me, he'll scarcely look on't.
Such a foe, good heavens!

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
I see a man's life is a tedious one:
I have tired myself, and for two nights together
Have made the ground my bed. I should be sick,
But that my resolution helps me. Milford,
When from the mountain-top Pisanio show'd thee,
Thou wast within a ken: O Jove! I think
Foundations fly the wretched; such, I mean,
Where they should be relieved. Two beggars told me
I could not miss my way: will poor folks lie,
That have afflictions on them, knowing 'tis
A punishment or trial? Yes; no wonder,
When rich ones scarce tell true. To lapse in fulness
Is sorer than to lie for need, and falsehood
Is worse in kings than beggars. My dear lord!
Thou art one o' the false ones. Now I think on thee,

Exit, to the cave
Enter BELARIUS, GUIDERIUS, and ARVIRAGUS

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
You, Polydore, have proved best woodman and
Are master of the feast: Cadwal and I
Will play the cook and servant; 'tis our match:
The sweat of industry would dry and die,
But for the end it works to. Come; our stomachs
Will make what's homely savoury: weariness
Can snore upon the flint, when resty sloth
Finds the down pillow hard. Now peace be here,
Poor house, that keep'st thyself!

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text: I am thoroughly weary.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text: I am weak with toil, yet strong in appetite.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
There is cold meat i' the cave; we'll browse on that,
Whilst what we have kill'd be cook'd.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
There is cold meat i' the cave; we'll browse on that,
Whilst what we have kill'd be cook'd.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
[Looking into the cave]
Stay; come not in.
But that it eats our victuals, I should think
Here were a fairy.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
[Looking into the cave]
Stay; come not in.
But that it eats our victuals, I should think
Here were a fairy.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text: What's the matter, sir?

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
By Jupiter, an angel! or, if not,
An earthly paragon! Behold divineness
No elder than a boy!

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
By Jupiter, an angel! or, if not,
An earthly paragon! Behold divineness
No elder than a boy!

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS

Re-enter IMOGEN

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Good masters, harm me not:
Before I enter'd here, I call'd; and thought
To have begg'd or bought what I have took:
good troth,
I have stol'n nought, nor would not, though I had found
Gold strew'd i' the floor. Here's money for my meat:
I would have left it on the board so soon
As I had made my meal, and parted
With prayers for the provider.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text: Money, youth?

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
All gold and silver rather turn to dirt!
As 'tis no better reckon'd, but of those
Who worship dirty gods.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
I see you're angry:
Know, if you kill me for my fault, I should
Have died had I not made it.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
I see you're angry:
Know, if you kill me for my fault, I should
Have died had I not made it.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text: Whither bound?

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text: To Milford-Haven.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text: What's your name?

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Fidele, sir. I have a kinsman who
Is bound for Italy; he embark'd at Milford;
To whom being going, almost spent with hunger,
I am fall'n in this offence.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
Prithee, fair youth,
Think us no churls, nor measure our good minds
By this rude place we live in. Well encounter'd!
'Tis almost night: you shall have better cheer
Ere you depart: and thanks to stay and eat it.
Boys, bid him welcome.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
Were you a woman, youth,
I should woo hard but be your groom. In honesty,
I bid for you as I'd buy.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
Were you a woman, youth,
I should woo hard but be your groom. In honesty,
I bid for you as I'd buy.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
I'll make't my comfort
He is a man; I'll love him as my brother:
And such a welcome as I'd give to him
After long absence, such is yours: most welcome!
Be sprightly, for you fall 'mongst friends.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN

Would it had been so, that they
Had been my father's sons! then had my prize
Been less, and so more equal ballasting
To thee, Posthumus.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text: He wrings at some distress.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text: Would I could free't!

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
Or I, whate'er it be,
What pain it cost, what danger. God's!

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
Or I, whate'er it be,
What pain it cost, what danger. God's!

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text: Hark, boys.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text: Hark, boys.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Great men,
That had a court no bigger than this cave,
That did attend themselves and had the virtue
Which their own conscience seal'd them—laying by
That nothing-gift of differing multitudes—
Could not out-peer these twain. Pardon me, gods!
I'd change my sex to be companion with them,
Since Leonatus's false.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
It shall be so.
Boys, we'll go dress our hunt. Fair youth, come in:
Discourse is heavy, fasting; when we have supp'd,
We'll mannerly demand thee of thy story,
So far as thou wilt speak it.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text: Pray, draw near.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
The night to the owl and morn to the lark
less welcome.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text: Thanks, sir.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text: I pray, draw near.
Exeunt
Enter two Senators and Tribunes

sentences.speaker: FIRST SENATOR
sentences.text:
This is the tenor of the emperor's writ:
That since the common men are now in action
'Gainst the Pannonians and Dalmatians,
And that the legions now in Gallia are
Full weak to undertake our wars against
The fall'n-off Britons, that we do incite
The gentry to this business. He creates
Lucius preconsul: and to you the tribunes,
For this immediate levy, he commends
His absolute commission. Long live Caesar!

sentences.speaker: FIRST TRIBUNE
sentences.text: Is Lucius general of the forces?

sentences.speaker: SECOND SENATOR
sentences.text: Ay.

sentences.speaker: FIRST TRIBUNE
sentences.text: Remaining now in Gallia?

sentences.speaker: FIRST SENATOR
sentences.text:
With those legions
Which I have spoke of, whereunto your levy
Must be supplyant: the words of your commission
Will tie you to the numbers and the time
Of their dispatch.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SENATOR
sentences.text:
With those legions
Which I have spoke of, whereunto your levy
Must be supplyant: the words of your commission
Will tie you to the numbers and the time
Of their dispatch.

sentences.speaker: FIRST TRIBUNE
sentences.text: We will discharge our duty.
Exeunt
Enter CLOTEN

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
I am near to the place where they should meet, if
Pisanio have mapped it truly. How fit his garments
serve me! Why should his mistress, who was made by
him that made the tailor, not be fit too? the
rather—saving reverence of the word—for 'tis said
a woman's fitness comes by fits. Therein I must
play the workman. I dare speak it to myself—for it
is not vain-glory for a man and his glass to confer
in his own chamber— I mean, the lines of my body are
as well drawn as his; no less young, more strong,
not beneath him in fortunes, beyond him in the
advantage of the time, above him in birth, alike
conversant in general services, and more remarkable
in single oppositions: yet this imperceiverant
thing loves him in my despite. What mortality is!
Posthumus, thy head, which now is growing upon thy
shoulders, shall within this hour be off; thy
mistress enforced; thy garments cut to pieces before
thy face: and all this done, spurn her home to her
father; who may haply be a little angry for my so
rough usage; but my mother, having power of his
testiness, shall turn all into my commendations. My
horse is tied up safe: out, sword, and to a sore
purpose! Fortune, put them into my hand! This is
the very description of their meeting-place; and
the fellow dares not deceive me.

Enter, from the cave, BELARIUS, GUIDERIUS, ARVIRAGUS, and IMOGEN

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
[To IMOGEN] You are not well: remain here in the cave;
We'll come to you after hunting.

Are we not brothers?

IMOGEN

GUIDERIUS
sentences.text: Go you to hunting; I'll abide with him.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text: Go you to hunting; I'll abide with him.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
So sick I am not, yet I am not well;
But not so citizen a wanton as
To seem to die ere sick: so please you, leave me;
Stick to your journal course: the breach of custom
Is breach of all. I am ill, but your being by me
Cannot amend me; society is no comfort
To one not sociable: I am not very sick,
Since I can reason of it. Pray you, trust me here:
I'll rob none but myself; and let me die,
Stealing so poorly.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
I love thee; I have spoke it
How much the quantity, the weight as much,
As I do love my father.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text: What! how! how!

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text: What! how! how!

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
If it be sin to say so, I yoke me
In my good brother's fault: I know not why
I love this youth; and I have heard you say,
Love's reason's without reason: the bier at door,
And a demand who is't shall die, I'd say
'My father, not this youth.'

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
[Aside] O noble strain!
O worthiness of nature! breed of greatness!
Cowards father cowards and base things sire base:
Nature hath meal and bran, contempt and grace.
I'm not their father; yet who this should be,
Doth miracle itself, loved before me.
'Tis the ninth hour o' the morn.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text: Brother, farewell.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text: I wish ye sport.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text: You health. So please you, sir.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text: You health. So please you, sir.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
[Aside] These are kind creatures. Gods, what lies
I have heard!
Our courtiers say all's savage but at court:
Experience, O, thou disprovest report!
The imperious seas breed monsters, for the dish
Poor tributary rivers as sweet fish.
I am sick still; heart-sick. Pisanio,
I'll now taste of thy drug.

Thus did he answer me: yet said, hereafter

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
To the field, to the field!
We'll leave you for this time: go in and rest.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text: We'll not be long away.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
Pray, be not sick,
For you must be our housewife.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
Pray, be not sick,
For you must be our housewife.

sentences.speaker: IMOGEN
sentences.text:
Well or ill,
I am bound to you.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text: And shalt be ever.
Exit IMOGEN, to the cave

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
This youth, how'er distress'd, appears he hath had
Good ancestors.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text: How angel-like he sings!

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
But his neat cookery! he cut our roots
In characters,
And sauced our broths, as Juno had been sick
And he her dieter.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
But his neat cookery! he cut our roots
In characters,
And sauced our broths, as Juno had been sick
And he her dieter.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
Nobly he yokes
A smiling with a sigh, as if the sigh
Was that it was, for not being such a smile;
The smile mocking the sigh, that it would fly
From so divine a temple, to commix
With winds that sailors rail at.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
Nobly he yokes
A smiling with a sigh, as if the sigh
Was that it was, for not being such a smile;
The smile mocking the sigh, that it would fly
From so divine a temple, to commix
With winds that sailors rail at.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
I do note
That grief and patience, rooted in him both,
Mingle their spurs together.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
Grow, patience!
And let the stinking elder, grief, untwine
His perishing root with the increasing vine!

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
Grow, patience!
And let the stinking elder, grief, untwine
His perishing root with the increasing vine!

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
It is great morning. Come, away!—
Who's there?

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
It is great morning. Come, away!—
Who's there?
Enter CLOTEN

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
I cannot find those runagates; that villain
Hath mock'd me. I am faint.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
I cannot find those runagates; that villain
Hath mock'd me. I am faint.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
I cannot find those runagates; that villain
Hath mock'd me. I am faint.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
'Those runagates!'
Means he not us? I partly know him: 'tis
Cloten, the son o' the queen. I fear some ambush.
I saw him not these many years, and yet
I know 'tis he. We are held as outlaws: hence!

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Soft! What are you
That fly me thus? some villain mountaineers?
I have heard of such. What slave art thou?

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
A thing
More slavish did I ne'er than answering
A slave without a knock.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Thou art a robber,
A law-breaker, a villain: yield thee, thief.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Thou art a robber,
A law-breaker, a villain: yield thee, thief.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
To who? to thee? What art thou? Have not I
An arm as big as thine? a heart as big?
Thy words, I grant, are bigger, for I wear not
My dagger in my mouth. Say what thou art,
Why I should yield to thee?

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Thou villain base,
Know'st me not by my clothes?

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
No, nor thy tailor, rascal,
Who is thy grandfather: he made those clothes,
Which, as it seems, make thee.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
No, nor thy tailor, rascal,
Who is thy grandfather: he made those clothes,
Which, as it seems, make thee.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Thou precious varlet,
My tailor made them not.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
GUIDERIUS
GUIDERIUS
CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Thou injurious thief,
Hear but my name, and tremble.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text: What's thy name?

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text: Cloten, thou villain.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
Cloten, thou double villain, be thy name,
I cannot tremble at it: were it Toad, or
Adder, Spider,
'Twould move me sooner.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
Cloten, thou double villain, be thy name,
I cannot tremble at it: were it Toad, or
Adder, Spider,
'Twould move me sooner.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
To thy further fear,
Nay, to thy mere confusion, thou shalt know
I am son to the queen.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
I am sorry for 't; not seeming
So worthy as thy birth.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
I am sorry for 't; not seeming
So worthy as thy birth.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text: Art not afeard?

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
Those that I reverence those I fear, the wise:
At fools I laugh, not fear them.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
Those that I reverence those I fear, the wise:
At fools I laugh, not fear them.

sentences.speaker: CLOTEN
sentences.text:
Die the death:
When I have slain thee with my proper hand,
I'll follow those that even now fled hence,
And on the gates of Lud's-town set your heads:
Yield, rustic mountaineer.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text: No companies abroad?

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text: None in the world: you did mistake him, sure.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text: None in the world: you did mistake him, sure.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
I cannot tell: long is it since I saw him,
But time hath nothing blurr'd those lines of favour
Which then he wore; the snatches in his voice,
And burst of speaking, were as his: I am absolute
'Twas very Cloten.

Which then he wore; the snatches in his voice,
And burst of speaking, were as his: I am absolute
'Twas very Cloten.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
In this place we left them:
I wish my brother make good time with him,
You say he is so fell.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
In this place we left them:
I wish my brother make good time with him,
You say he is so fell.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
Being scarce made up,
I mean, to man, he had not apprehension
Of roaring terrors; for the effect of judgment
Is oft the cause of fear. But, see, thy brother.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
This Cloten was a fool, an empty purse;
There was no money in't: not Hercules
Could have knock'd out his brains, for he had none:
Yet I not doing this, the fool had borne
My head as I do his.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text: What hast thou done?

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
I am perfect what: cut off one Cloten's head,
Son to the queen, after his own report;
Who call'd me traitor, mountaineer, and swore
With his own single hand he'ld take us in
Displace our heads where—thank the gods!—they grow,
And set them on Lud's-town.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:

Displace our heads where—thank the gods!—they grow,
And set them on Lud's-town.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text: We are all undone.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
Why, worthy father, what have we to lose,
But that he swore to take, our lives? The law
Protects not us: then why should we be tender
To let an arrogant piece of flesh threat us,
Play judge and executioner all himself,
For we do fear the law? What company
Discover you abroad?

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
No single soul
Can we set eye on; but in all safe reason
He must have some attendants. Though his humour
Was nothing but mutation, ay, and that
From one bad thing to worse; not frenzy, not
Absolute madness could so far have raved
To bring him here alone; although perhaps
It may be heard at court that such as we
Cave here, hunt here, are outlaws, and in time
May make some stronger head; the which he hearing—
As it is like him—might break out, and swear
He'ld fetch us in; yet is't not probable
To come alone, either he so undertaking,
Or they so suffering: then on good ground we fear,
If we do fear this body hath a tail
More perilous than the head.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
Let ordinance
Come as the gods foresay it: howsoe'er,
My brother hath done well.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
Let ordinance
Come as the gods foresay it: howsoe'er,
My brother hath done well.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
I had no mind
To hunt this day: the boy Fidele's sickness
Did make my way long forth.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
I had no mind
To hunt this day: the boy Fidele's sickness
Did make my way long forth.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
With his own sword,
Which he did wave against my throat, I have ta'en
His head from him: I'll throw't into the creek
Behind our rock; and let it to the sea,
And tell the fishes he's the queen's son, Cloten:
That's all I reck.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
I fear 'twill be revenged:
Would, Polydote, thou hadst not done't! though valour
Becomes thee well enough.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
Would I had done't
So the revenge alone pursued me! Polydore,
I love thee brotherly, but envy much
Thou hast robb'd me of this deed: I would revenges,
That possible strength might meet, would seek us
through
And put us to our answer.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
Well, 'tis done:
We'll hunt no more to-day, nor seek for danger
Where there's no profit. I prithee, to our rock;
You and Fidele play the cooks: I'll stay
Till hasty Polydore return, and bring him
To dinner presently.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
Poor sick Fidele!
I'll weringly to him: to gain his colour
I'ld let a parish of such Clotens' blood,
And praise myself for charity.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
O thou goddess,
Thou divine Nature, how thyself thou blazon'st
In these two princely boys! They are as gentle
As zephyrs blowing below the violet,
Not wagging his sweet head; and yet as rough,
Their royal blood enchafed, as the rudest wind,
That by the top doth take the mountain pine,
And make him stoop to the vale. 'Tis wonder
That an invisible instinct should frame them
To royalty unlearn'd, honour untaught,
Civility not seen from other, valour
That wildly grows in them, but yields a crop
As if it had been sow'd. Yet still it's strange
What Cloten's being here to us portends,
Or what his death will bring us.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
Where's my brother?
I have sent Cloten's clotpoll down the stream,
In embassy to his mother: his body's hostage
For his return.

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
My ingenious instrument!
Hark, Polydore, it sounds! But what occasion
Hath Cadwal now to give it motion? Hark!

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text: Is he at home?

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text: He went hence even now.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
What does he mean? since death of my dear'st mother
it did not speak before. All solemn things
Should answer solemn accidents. The matter?
Triumphs for nothing and lamenting toys
Is jollity for apes and grief for boys.
Is Cadwal mad?

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS

Look, here he comes,
And brings the dire occasion in his arms
Of what we blame him for.
Re-enter ARVIRAGUS, with IMOGEN, as dead, bearing her in his arms

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
The bird is dead
That we have made so much on. I had rather
Have skipp'd from sixteen years of age to sixty,
To have turn'd my leaping-time into a crutch,
Than have seen this.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
O sweetest, fairest lily!
My brother wears thee not the one half so well
As when thou grew'st thyself.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
O sweetest, fairest lily!
My brother wears thee not the one half so well

sentences.speaker: BELARIUS
sentences.text:
O melancholy!
Who ever yet could sound thy bottom? find
The ooze, to show what coast thy sluggish crare
Might easiliest harbour in? Thou blessed thing!
Jove knows what man thou mightst have made; but I,
Thou diedst, a most rare boy, of melancholy.
How found you him?

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
Stark, as you see:
Thus smiling, as some fly hid tickled slumber,
Not as death's dart, being laugh'd at; his
right cheek
Reposing on a cushion.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text: Where?

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
O' the floor;
His arms thus leagued: I thought he slept, and put
My clouted brogues from off my feet, whose rudeness
Answer'd my steps too loud.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
Why, he but sleeps:
If he be gone, he'll make his grave a bed;
With female fairies will his tomb be haunted,
And worms will not come to thee.

sentences.speaker: ARVIRAGUS
sentences.text:
With fairest flowers
Whilst summer lasts and I live here, Fidele,
I'll sweeten thy sad grave: thou shalt not lack
The flower that's like thy face, pale primrose, nor
The azured harebell, like thy veins, no, nor
The leaf of eglantine, whom not to slander,
Out-sweeten'd not thy breath: the ruddock would,
With charitable bill,—O bill, sore-shaming
Those rich-left heirs that let their fathers lie
Without a monument!—bring thee all this;
Yea, and furr'd moss besides, when flowers are none,
To winter-ground thy corse.

sentences.speaker: GUIDERIUS
sentences.text:
Prithee, have done;
And do not play in wench-like words with that
Which is so serious. Let us bury him,
And not protract with admiration what
Is now due debt. To the grave!
